races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free EBF Maiden Stakes (Newcomers' Race) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Romeo Guest
runners.spotlight: March foal; £26,000 yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Miss Attitude (5f Listed; RPR 112), The Good Biscuit (5f/6f; 85), Sporting Icon (6f; 74) and Laura's Legacy (German 7f/1m); dam 5f winner (87); bred to be sharp, stable is in form and Tom Marquand an encouraging booking.
runners.comment: Bred to be sharp, stable is in form and Tom Marquand looks a notable booking

runners.name: Harry Knows
runners.spotlight: Mehmas colt who was foaled in March and cost 40,000euros as a yearling; tenth foal; half-brother to winners Moonlight In Paris (1m/8.6f; RPR 103), Malayan (7f 2yo; 87), Maison De York (6f-1m; 86) and Autumn Rose (multiple 7f; 71); dam 1m1f/9.4f winner (94); trainer had a well-touted newcomer collect at the first time of asking on the AW last week; interesting under Oisin Murphy.
runners.comment: Mehmas colt who was foaled in March and cost 40,000euros as a yearling
runners.owner: Thames Boys, J Henwood & J Clay

runners.name: From Me To You
runners.spotlight: January foal; £55,000 yearling by Persian Force who won the Brocklesby on debut for this yard; third foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Theophilos (1m4f Flat/2m hurdle), Tabernas (UAE 6f/Oman 1m2f) and She's All In Gold (2m3f hurdle); trainer's 2yos already off the mark and he won this in 2023 and 2024, and had the close runner-up 12 months ago; of obvious interest.
runners.comment: January foal; £55,000 yearling by Persian Force; stable has a notable record in this race

runners.name: Blessed Voyager
runners.spotlight: April foal; 80,000euros yearling by Bayside Boy who made a winning debut at this track; dam a Group-class 5.7f-1m winner; strong yard, which saddled the runner-up in the Brocklesby, is likely to be rich in 2yo talent.
runners.comment: April foal; second foal out of a Group-class 5.7f-1m winner; from a powerful yard

runners.name: Crusherman
runners.spotlight: February foal; 52,000euros yearling; third foal; half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Dial Me In (RPR 85); dam twice-raced half-sister to Listed winners Daktani (1m 2yo), Diatribe (6f/7f) and Diaphora (1m); stable's newcomers are notorious for improving from their debut runs.
runners.comment: Stable's newcomers are notorious for improving from their debut runs

runners.name: Drum Major
runners.spotlight: February foal; 15,000euros yearling; tenth foal; half-brother to winners Shanghai Dragon (1m; RPR 93), Eyelool (7f turf/1m AW; 88), Petticoat Rule (7f/1m; 79) and Kylie Of Lochalsh (6f 2yo; 72); dam placed 5.7f (51); likely best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: February foal; 15,000euros yearling; best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise

runners.name: El Lambo
runners.spotlight: April foal; 20,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to four winners including Electric Ladyland (5f; RPR 94), Thegreatestshowman (5f-6f; 92) and Simmy's Copshop (5f-1m; 90); dam 7f/1m AW winner (80); precocious pedigree and any market confidence would naturally heighten interest; stable's first 2yo runner of the season.
runners.comment: April foal; 20,000gns yearling; precocious pedigree; market should be instructive
runners.owner: The Lamb Inn Wantage Racing Team

runners.name: Jaan Ki Tukri
runners.spotlight: April foal by Kodiac; 140,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to US 1m turf winner Vina Arana (RPR 102); dam Greek 6.5f winner, half-sister to winners Path Of Thunder (Listed-placed 7f/1m) and Excellent Sunset (US Graded stakes-placed 1m); first 2yo runner of the season from a yard that does well in early juvenile races (won this last year).
runners.comment: April foal by Kodiac; 140,000gns yearling; stable won this 12 months ago

runners.name: Napa
runners.spotlight: March foal; 68,000gns yearling; ninth foal; closely related to winners Pentle Bay (Listed-placed 6f 2yo; RPR 87) and Japarana (French 1m3f/12.5f) and half-brother to four winners including Berkshire Boom (7f 2yo; 72); dam French 1m4f winner; hard to know what his trip will be but stable's only 2yo runner this season was a winner.
runners.comment: Hard to gauge what his trip will be but stable's only 2yo runner this season was a winner

runners.name: Ricky's Rocket
runners.spotlight: Late January foal; 20,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Prop Forward (5f-6f; RPR 108), Head For Freedom (5f 2yo; 66) and She Looks Like Fun (Italian 7f/1m); dam 5f/6f winner, Listed class; stable 0-5 with 2yos this season.
runners.comment: Late January foal; 20,000gns yearling; dam 5f/6f winner, Listed class

races.race_name: Bridget 'Confined' Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Newcomers' Race) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Song Of The Clouds
runners.spotlight: By Night Of Thunder; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Mighty Ulysses (7f/1m including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 117) and Lou Lou's Gift (6f AW 2yo/7f turf; 102); dam 7f/1m winner (including 2yo/Listed; 108), out of half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Rebecca Sharp; bred to be useful and the stable's recent record in this race (won in 2022, 2023 (one division) and 2025) piques interest.
runners.comment: Half-sister to Group 3 winner Mighty Ulysses; stable took this in 2022, 2023 and 2025

runners.name: Bright Summer
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; by Dark Angel closely related to 5f/6f winner Star Chorus (including AW; RPR 92), half-sister to winners See That Storm (1m/1m2f including 2yo/AW; 103) and Bell Song (6f-1m AW; 88); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 93); stable saddled the runner-up in this race 12 months ago.
runners.comment: She has three useful winning siblings & yard sent out the 2nd here last year; likely type

runners.name: Lucky Luna
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; by Lucky Vega; eighth foal; closely related to 1m2f winner Capla Lazarus (RPR 85), half-sister to French winners Salt Lake City (5f-1m including 2yo/AW; 97) and Flying Fleur (1m1f); dam unraced half-sister to Listed winners Wilki (6f 2yo) and Madhya (1m); trainer, who runs three here, won this race in 2019 (with perceived second string) and 2024.
runners.comment: 40,000gns yearling with three winning siblings; one of three runners for Richard Hannon

runners.name: Seet
runners.spotlight: 425,000gns yearling; by Too Darn Hot; tenth foal; half-sister to five winners including Kalpana (8.6f-1m4f including AW/Group 1; RPR 122), Floating Spirit (7f/1m; 87) and Vividly (7f-1m1f including 2yo/US; 82); dam 9.5f-1m4f AW winner (including Listed; 101), sister to 1m4f Group 1 winner Zambezi Sun; will stay further but she has plenty to recommend her on paper.
runners.comment: 425,000gns half-sister to 5 winners including Group 1 scorer Kalpana; strong paper claims

runners.name: Pierpoint
runners.spotlight: 80,000gns foal; by Palace Pier; sixth foal; closely related to 8.6f/1m2f AW winner You Saw Brigadoon (RPR 63), half-sister to three winners including Archies Lad (7f; 74) and Canzone (5f AW; 71); dam Listed-placed 7f-7.5f winner (104), half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Balisada; one of three runners for Richard Hannon.
runners.comment: 80,000gns foal; has four winning siblings and dam was useful; one of three for the stable

runners.name: Tarrant
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns foal; by Space Blues; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Viareggio (7f-1m1f including 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 113) and Just Bring It (1m-1m3f including AW/Qatar; 99); dam 1m winner (89), half-sister to 1m2f Qatar Derby winner Tannaaf; one of three runners for Richard Hannon and the choice of Sean Levey.
runners.comment: 75,000gns half-sister to two useful winners; looks the Hannon first string

runners.name: Akademy Lady
runners.spotlight: 200,000euros yearling; by No Nay Never; ninth foal; half-sister to winners Long Tradition (1m/1m2f AW; RPR 104) and Imperiality (6f 2yo; 77); dam Group-placed 1m winner (105), closely related to 1m Group 2 winner Echo Of Light; lots to like on pedigree.
runners.comment: 200,000euros half-sister to two winners out of a well-related Group-placed 1m winner

runners.name: Bundok
runners.spotlight: 52,000gns yearling; by Pinatubo; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Smart Vision (5f/7f including AW; RPR 95) and Fragrance (5f 2yo; 75); dam 6f 2yo/7f winner (91), half-sister to smart 6f-1m winner Upper Hand; should have a future but others make greater appeal today; stable also runs Mother Dear.
runners.comment: 52,000gns half-sister to two winners out of a 6f-7f winner; perhaps a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Cap Santa Lucia
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling; by Persian King; fourth foal; sister to 1m 2yo/10.5f winner Cap Saint Martin (RPR 96), half-sister to winners Dire La Verite (1m AW; 92) and Cap San Roman (French 10.5f/1m3f); dam French 1m4f (Listed) and 12.5f winner (100); 7f perhaps on the sharp side judged on pedigree.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; has three winning siblings but bred to come into her own at 1m+

runners.name: Mother Dear
runners.spotlight: By Masar; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Treasure (1m 2yo; RPR 104) and De La Treble (Italian 7.5f 2yo); dam placed 1m2f (71), half-sister to winners Mandaean (1m2f 2yo Group 1) and Wavering (1m2f Group 1); one of two runners for Hughie Morrison; may need more of a test in time.
runners.comment: Masar half-sister to two winners; bred to need more of a test in time; stable runs two

runners.name: Sapphire Secret
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling; by Kingman; eleventh foal; half-sister to five winners including Delius (1m1f-1m4f including Australian Group 1; RPR 127), Juan Elcano (7f 2yo/1m2f Listed; 115) and Nkosikazi (1m-1m2f including AW Group 3; 106); dam maiden (68) sister to 7f 2yo Listed winner Whazzat; looks the part on paper and the betting should reveal more.
runners.comment: 420,000gns half-sister to 5 winners, notably Australian Gr 1 winner Delius; market useful

runners.name: Synchronicity
runners.spotlight: 900,000gns yearling; by Night Of Thunder; third foal; sister to 1m-10.3f winner Ombudsman (including Group 1s; RPR 130), half-sister to 7f winner American Belle (83); dam 7f AW 2yo/1m2f turf winner (79); holds an Irish 1000 Guineas entry; purchase price/pedigree both catch the eye.
runners.comment: 900,000gns sister to top-class Ombudsman; Irish 1000 Guineas entrant; appealing pedigree

races.race_name: Dreweatts Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Light Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners, easily the best being Bolthole (1m-1m3f; RPR 116); weak 12-1 for seven-runner maiden at Kempton (7f, AW) 16 days ago but came from the back to go down by a neck; experienced winner (hot favourite) represented same connections as Allaire; improvement is almost certainly needed but that's the best form in this field and a bright start to career; yard also runs Semper Femina.
runners.comment: Weak 12-1 but lots of promise when neck 2nd from off pace at Kempton (7f, AW) 16 days ago

runners.name: Allaire
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners including Eldar Eldarov (1m-14.5f including St Leger; RPR 117) and Kingswood (1m1f/1m2f; 113); 7-2 from 17-2 for novice at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and came from towards rear to be second of 11; looks sure to improve, with 1m2f a help.
runners.comment: 7-2 from 17-2 at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and came from towards rear to be runner-up

runners.name: Semper Femina
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros yearling by Lucky Vega, who's also the sire of stablemate Light Dreamer; well-backed second favourite for maiden at Kempton (1m3f, AW) 18 days ago when slowly into stride but coming from the rear to be second of eight; should build on that and the market moves should be interesting.
runners.comment: Second favourite, slow-starting second of eight at Kempton (1m3f, AW) 18 days ago

runners.name: Tempestra
runners.spotlight: 360,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; fourth foal; sister to winners Sea On Time (11.6f/1m4f; RPR 110), Sea Just In Time (1m2f 2yo/1m4f; 101) and Truthful (1m2f/11.6f; 100); dam unraced half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner, out of 1m2f Group 3 winner; blinkers are notable on debut but she needs a close look.
runners.comment: 360,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; sister to smart/useful winners; blinkered on debut

runners.name: I'm The One
runners.spotlight: By Sea The Stars; first foal; dam German 1m2f/1m4f winner (including Group 1; RPR 112), half-sister to German 1m3f Listed winner, out of half-sister to German 1m3f Group 2 winner Simoun; Oaks entry from a top yard.
runners.comment: By Sea The Stars; dam German 1m2f/1m4f winner (including Group 1; RPR 112); Oaks entry

runners.name: Wild Violet
runners.spotlight: By Zarak; first foal; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 79) out of French 9.7f-winning half-sister to useful 6f 2yo/1m winner Ershaad, is granddaughter of Canadian 1m2f Grade 1 winner; late foal; another to check out.
runners.comment: By Zarak; first foal; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 79); late foal

runners.name: Eze Sur Mer
runners.spotlight: Useful pedigree; fourth at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in December and March but this race surely demands a lot more.
runners.comment: Fourth at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in December and March but surely needs much better

runners.name: Bintnaseefa
runners.spotlight: 220,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; third foal; half-sister to 7f winner Triumph Of Peace (RPR 83); dam 7f winner (including Listed; 102), half-sister to fairly useful winners, out of 5f 2yo Listed winner; market can guide, including as her stable also runs Take A View.
runners.comment: 220,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; stable also runs Take A View; market can guide

runners.name: Marianita
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling by Lope De Vega; sixth foal; sister to 10.6f AW winner Sacred Oath (RPR 80), half-sister to winners Expand The Map (US 1m turf) and Verdant Lady (French 12.5f AW); dam French 1m winner (109) out of French Group-placed 1m winner; powerful yard also runs Musical Accord.
runners.comment: 200,000gns yearling by Lope De Vega; powerful yard also runs Musical Accord

runners.name: Musical Accord
runners.spotlight: 350,000gns yearling by Camelot; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Laneqash (7f/1m including Listed; RPR 115), Open Mind (6f/7f; 101), Miss Oxford (French 7f; 93) and Aysgarth (7f 2yo; 85); dam 8.6f AW winner (72); from a leading yard which also runs Marianita, so the market can give big clues.
runners.comment: 350,000gns yearling by Camelot; from a leading yard which also runs Marianita

runners.name: Take A View
runners.spotlight: By Sea The Stars; fifth foal; sister to 7f/1m2f 2yo winner One World (RPR 91), half-sister to winners Time Lock (1m4f including Group 3; 113) and Continuite (1m; 93); dam 1m-1m2f winner (96), sister to 1m/1m2f Group 2 winner Time Test; stable also runs Bintnaseefa and the market can probably guide.
runners.comment: By Sea The Stars; sister to One World (RPR 91) and half-sister to Time Lock (113)

runners.name: Society Girl
runners.spotlight: 33-1 at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft) in October and looked to be struggling for a long way, but she stayed on for an eye-catching fourth of ten; disappointing, however, when 9-2 at Newcastle (1m, AW) four weeks later.
runners.comment: Eye-catching when 33-1 at Yarmouth (1m) in October; disappointing on AW four weeks later

runners.name: Morningtoncrescent
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns yearling by Bated Breath; first foal; dam 1m4f/13.5f winner (RPR 90) out of 1m Listed-winning half-sister to 2,000 Guineas winner Footstepsinthesand; 14-1, always mid-division when beaten 14l in maiden at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in December; major work to do.
runners.comment: 14-1, always mid-division when beaten 14l at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in December
runners.owner: Emma Banks , Rocco, De Ramsey & Dobson

runners.name: Tenison
runners.spotlight: 200-1 and 150-1 when towards rear at Newmarket (7f, good) and Kempton (1m, AW) in the autumn.
runners.comment: 200-1 and 150-1 when towards rear at Newmarket (7f, good) and Kempton (1m, AW) as 2yo

runners.name: Paean Of Appin
runners.spotlight: 25-1, ducked left from inside stall when always behind in maiden here (6f, good to soft) in September; has an extra 4f but needs a transformation.
runners.comment: Always behind here (6f, good to soft; 25-1) in September, ducking left from inside stall

races.race_name: Darley EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Shabab Al Ahli
runners.spotlight: Dante and Derby entry; 500,000gns yearling; Frankel close relative of 1m3f-1m6f winner Klondike (Group 3; RPR 115) and a half-brother to Kemari (11.4f-1m6f Group 3; RPR 114); 8-15 for novice at Southwell (1m3f, AW) in February, in just a four-runner race but he left another Frankel colt just over 8l behind in second; looked green but with lots of scope, a good prospect.
runners.comment: Dante and Derby entry; green but lots of scope when Southwell winner (1m3f, AW) February

runners.name: Maltese Cross
runners.spotlight: Dante and Derby entry; went close on debut at Ascot (1m, soft) and one better in a Newmarket maiden (1m, good) later in September; battled back admirably in the latter, which is strong form; looks a good prospect for 1m2f and probably even more so for 1m4f.
runners.comment: Dante and Derby entry; went close at Ascot (1m) and one better in a Newmarket maiden (1m)

runners.name: Alfaraz
runners.spotlight: Dante and Derby entry; 300,000gns yearling by Nathaniel; second as 2yo in small fields for a novice event at Goodwood (1m, good to firm) and conditions stakes at Epsom (8.5f, good); had no answers in the latter to a subsequent Listed winner but stuck to his task well and 1m2f+ will suit.
runners.comment: Dante and Derby entry; second on both starts as 2yo; shaped as if 1m2f+ will suit

runners.name: Joulany
runners.spotlight: Derby entry; 800,000gns Sea The Stars yearling, brother to multiple Group 3 winner Al Aasy (1m2f-1m5f); turned over at odds-on in a novice at Kempton (7f, AW) and maiden at York (1m, good) last autumn, with debut the better effort; hooded for reappearance; has plenty of ability and should win before long but others in this have not blotted their copybooks.
runners.comment: Derby entry; beaten when odds-on at Kempton (7f; better effort) and York (1m) last autumn
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Amo Racing & Valmont

runners.name: Sahara King
runners.spotlight: 16-1 for ten-runner novice at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in November, but always prominent and led final strides from Ranga Tang who ran in the 1m2f Newmarket handicap on Wednesday; does not have the most striking pedigree for one in these colours but looks sure to stay 1m2f and improve.
runners.comment: 16-1 at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in November but always prominent and led final strides
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Much
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; Frankel close relation to 11.6f/1m4f winner Flower Of Scotland (RPR 87), half-brother to winners Promissory (Group-placed 1m4f; 104) and Royal Scandal (1m2f/1m4f AW; 98); dam 1m3f-1m4f winner (including Group 1; 118); tongue tied for debut but it will be interesting to see his market strength.
runners.comment: By Frankel; tongue tied for debut but it will be interesting to see his market strength

runners.name: Easwrith Destiny
runners.spotlight: By Leading Light; first foal; dam pulled up in a bumper on her only start, half-sister to French 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Stefer, is granddaughter of US 1m3f Grade 3 winner; makes his debut in what should be an extremely hot race, so is probably best watched.
runners.comment: By Leading Light; first foal; dam pulled up in bumper on her only start; debut in hot race

runners.name: My Love Is King
runners.spotlight: Dante and Derby entry; brother to US 1m-1m5f winner Serve The King (including turf Grade 2; RPR 115), half-brother to three winners including Loving Things (1m2f-1m4f including Group 3; 109); 4-6 for five-runner novice at Newcastle (7f, AW) in November, running green but ending comfortably on top; fascinating, pitched into strong race for this second start.
runners.comment: Dante and Derby entry; 4-6 at Newcastle (7f, AW; five ran) in November, comfortably on top

runners.name: Barrister
runners.spotlight: Derby entry by Without Parole; 14-1, close up long way when fourth of 12 in novice at Kempton (7f, AW) in October; should improve but pedigree and that performance are not nearly as striking as some in this race.
runners.comment: Derby entry; 14-1, fourth of 12 in novice at Kempton (7f, AW) in October

runners.name: Ataturk
runners.spotlight: Golden Horn half-brother to the same connections' smart stayer Kyle Of Lochalsh; never dangerous at 50-1 for a novice here (1m, soft) in October and he's still likely to come into his own later and over further.
runners.comment: Never dangerous at 50-1 here (1m, soft) in October; likely to come into his own later on

races.race_name: cityam.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Pole Star
runners.spotlight: First five handicaps were all over about 1m6f, with two wins (good/soft) and two respectable efforts in valuable races in late summer; just 10-1 for 2m2f Cesarewitch at Newmarket on final outing, which did not work out but he has been gelded and could still have more to offer in this second season.
runners.comment: 2m2f Cesarewitch (10-1) did not work out; gelded since and could still have more to offer
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Classical Allusion
runners.spotlight: Won Southwell maiden (1m4f, AW) in September on debut; second favourite to superstar hurdler Constitution Hill in Kempton novice (1m4f, AW) last month and chased him home 2l adrift, perhaps giving him more to think about than had widely been expected; not easy to pin down but 2m is plausible and his connections presumably like the look of his opening handicap mark.
runners.comment: Won debut; kept superstar hurdler Constitution Hill up to his work at Kempton (1m4f, AW)

runners.name: Lavender Hill Mob
runners.spotlight: 1m4f winner from the front (under Mason Paetel) at Newmarket last May who also scored with a prominent ride over hurdles at Cheltenham (2m5f) in November; had a bit to prove on AW (and over 2m on Flat) when always behind at Kempton three weeks ago but should be benefited from that run.
runners.comment: Always behind at Kempton three weeks ago but should be benefited from that run

runners.name: Caprelo
runners.spotlight: Probably acts on any going but peak efforts were on AW at Kempton (2m) where he won in September and was runner-up in December; chance on that but needs to improve his turf form.
runners.comment: Peak efforts on last two AW starts; chance on that but needs to improve his turf form

runners.name: Francesco Baracca
runners.spotlight: Has done best on AW, including three wins at Wolverhampton (1m6f/2m) in the last six months; well held at Lingfield two weeks ago but has a modest record there; has won twice on turf, latterly on good ground at Bath (1m6f) last June but none of his turf form matches today's mark.
runners.comment: Three AW wins in the last six months but none of his turf form matches today's mark

runners.name: Call My Bluff
runners.spotlight: 5-25; second in this race in 2023 and close third in the Chester Cup (2m2f, again on soft; favourite off 9lb higher than today) that May but absent for 758 days afterwards; while not disgraced on reappearance last October, he then tailed off on AW last month; major questions to answer regarding the ground and what he's currently capable of.
runners.comment: Major questions to answer regarding the ground and what he's currently capable of

runners.name: Blazeon Five
runners.spotlight: Ended last year in storming form for 1m6f/2m hat-trick and not disgraced when never dangerous in January when last seen; peak efforts on softer than good but she's not dismissed.
runners.comment: Ended last year in storming form trick and not disgraced in January latest; not dismissed

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Full Of Surprises Handicap

runners.name: Crown Office
runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut at Newmarket (6f, good to firm; strong form) last August; plenty went wrong at York on his second start and he showed his true colours when comfortably on top at the finish at Leicester when last seen in October; appeared to win that race in spite of the drop back to 6f and soft ground and he brings considerable potential to this handicap debut.
runners.comment: 6f maiden winner as a 2yo; brings untapped potential to this handicap debut; strong claims

runners.name: Tamashal
runners.spotlight: Major eyecatcher on debut and he has improved with each subsequent run, winning a 1m maiden at Chelmsford and following up in a 7f handicap at Southwell three weeks ago; up 3lb against stronger opposition this time.
runners.comment: Improving run by run; into a stronger race for his turf debut, so needs to progress again

runners.name: On The Inlet
runners.spotlight: Gelded after his three 2yo runs and proved a different proposition in two handicap runs this month, running away with a Class 5 at Southwell (1m) two weeks ago and following up at Wolverhampton (7f; Class 6) eight days ago; 6lb well in under his penalty but he is taking on a much higher calibre of opponent this afternoon.
runners.comment: Two bloodless handicap wins this month; 6lb well in but taking on much stiffer opposition

runners.name: Stellar Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt as a 2yo when seeing off 14 rivals at Glorious Goodwood (6f, good); improved for the step up to 7f when impressing in an 18-runner nursery at York's Ebor meeting (good to firm); only 5-1 for a Group 3 over C&D when last seen in October but he dropped away late on soft ground that may not have been ideal; could still have more to offer back on a sounder surface.
runners.comment: Below par in Gr 3 on soft when last seen but improving rapidly beforehand; may do better

runners.name: The Resdev Scholar
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in fine form, completing a hat-trick when powering through the mud at Doncaster in November (6f); promising second behind Tamashal at Southwell on return three weeks ago; in deeper waters today.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 on a roll and ran well on his return; this looks tougher though
runners.owner: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Illy's Roo
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Jack Channon; successful stable debut at Southwell last month and ran another solid race when third behind two reopposing rivals at Southwell three weeks ago; this race is tougher.
runners.comment: Two good runs for his new yard but this looks a much tougher assignment

runners.name: Caballo Grande
runners.spotlight: Inexperience evident on his 2yo debut (6f, good) and he has since made all for easy AW wins at Southwell (6f) and Wolverhampton (7f); the form of neither of those wins looks especially strong but he was in a different class to his rivals; this will be a far stiffer test of his credentials.
runners.comment: Easy AW wins from the front (6f-7f) on last two starts; this looks a much stiffer test

runners.name: Colori Forever
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful form as a 2yo, including winning debut, but took his form to a new level with a runaway success at Southwell (7f, AW) on return 12 days ago; this is a stronger race but he is 6lb well in under the penalty and the money that came for him at Southwell strongly suggested he was thought to be smart; stall 1 is a potential query but lots to like otherwise.
runners.comment: Heavily backed and won by wide margin at Southwell 12 days ago; 6lb well in under penalty

runners.name: Peel Park
runners.spotlight: 6f maiden win last summer; unplaced in good nurseries at York and Doncaster when last seen; gelded during his absence; needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: 6f maiden win was followed by two nursery defeats; improvement required after a gelding op

runners.name: Sapphire Steps
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of a 6f fillies' maiden here on debut last July; came up short in stronger company in three subsequent runs (5f-7f); absent for 237 days and she returns in a warm handicap; stable also runs Tamashal.
runners.comment: 6f win here on debut; came up short in stronger company afterwards; returns in warm race
runners.owner: J Palmer-Brown, C Engel And Partner

runners.name: May Angel
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of a 7f novice at Southwell on his second start; came up short in two Group 3 events afterwards (tried in a hood on second occasion); he has since undergone wind and gelding operations; hard pressed to describe him as well handicapped on what we have seen but the surgeries could unlock more.
runners.comment: Easy 7f AW win last summer before down the field in two Group 3s; wind/gelding ops since

runners.name: Astrazar
runners.spotlight: Had become exposed over sprint trips but his form took off when upped to 7f+ on his final two runs as a 2yo, following his Ffos Las maiden win with a last-ditch success in a York sales race in October; gelded since; making his handicap debut on his eighth start but he looks on an appealing mark.
runners.comment: 2-2 over 7f+; gelded since winning York sales race in October; interesting on h'cap debut

runners.name: Amorim
runners.spotlight: Raced over 6f as a 2yo, winning a Windsor novice by daylight last June and third in Listed race here three weeks later; soft ground excuses his poor run when last seen; improvement required for the longer trip and/or a gelding operation if he is to win today.
runners.comment: Promise over 6f as a 2yo; gelded since last seen; improvement required for the new trip
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Shaman Champion
runners.spotlight: Easy win on his debut (5f, good to firm) and showed fairly useful ability in five subsequent starts for Richard Hughes, including a C&D second when last seen in September (solid form); sold for 160,000gns the following month; begins this campaign up another 3lb; big personal best is required on his first run for a trainer who won this race in 2022 and 2023.
runners.comment: Pretty useful as 2yo, including C&D; sold 160,000gns since latest; personal best required

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Handicap

runners.name: Cindy Lou Who
runners.spotlight: Made giant strides for Tony Carroll last season, winning four times (three of them at Bath); confirmed her affinity for Bath, as well as the fact that she had done well over the winter, when short-headed in a Bath Listed race on Easter Sunday (5f, good); 15lb well in on this return to handicap company; obvious chance.
runners.comment: Progressive last year and reappearance Listed 2nd was better; 15lb well in; obvious claims

runners.name: Montezin
runners.spotlight: 320,000euros yearling but didn't reach the track for Godolphin and sold for 16,000gns in January 2025; three promising runs on AW in late 2025, second at Wolverhampton and Newcastle (both 5f) before justifying odds-on favouritism at Newcastle when upped to 6f in November; could have been found easier opportunities for his handicap debut and this is his trainer's first runner at Newbury; those factors both heighten interest in this unexposed 4yo.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW in late 2025; it's interesting that he makes his handicap debut here

runners.name: Brosay
runners.spotlight: 6f wins at Windsor and Ascot last summer and he's of interest on the latter effort; 33-1 and offered little in a 5f handicap at Newcastle four weeks ago; should be sharper today but others bring more compelling claims.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last summer; low-key return at Newcastle last month; others look safer

runners.name: Brian
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeats on his last three starts, including his Kempton reappearance three weeks ago; he's on a fair mark on the pick of his efforts though and this track does seem to suit him well; Tom Marquand is a notable booking for the stable (his nine rides for Stan Moore have yielded two wins, a second, three thirds and two fourths); lively outsider.
runners.comment: Well beaten on last three starts but goes well here and Tom Marquand is a notable booking

runners.name: Change Sings
runners.spotlight: Getting on for two years since his last win but he was a good second in this race last year (off 2lb higher mark) and he returned to action with a promising third at Kempton three weeks ago; solid contender.
runners.comment: 2nd in this race last year and 2lb lower today; solid 3rd at Kempton on return; contender

runners.name: Lakers
runners.spotlight: Unexposed at 6f; this mark is in range judged on his two placed efforts this winter; failed to fire on Good Friday but he received a kick before the start which won't have helped; no surprise to see him bounce back.
runners.comment: Below par on Good Friday (kicked before start) but progressive over this trip beforehand

runners.name: Roach Power
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a revival in the second half of 2025, winning five 5f handicaps on good ground or slower; no show over 6f when last seen in November and he returns to action off a tough mark.
runners.comment: Flourished in the second half of 2025 but over 5f; returns from a break off a tough mark

runners.name: Daring Legend
runners.spotlight: 40-1 for his stable debut at Kempton (6f, AW) last month but he ran a cracker for second, beaten a nose; up in grade today but not out of it on last year's turf best.
runners.comment: Good 2nd on stable debut last month; up in class but not out of it

runners.name: Mirabeau
runners.spotlight: Campaigned over as far as 10.3f and this will be his first 6f run since his 2yo debut; well beaten in the Lincoln on his reappearance (1m, good to soft); dangerous mark judged on best 2025 effort (7f, good to soft); trip is a query but Oisin Murphy a notable booking.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark if the drop back to sprinting works the oracle; Oisin Murphy booked
runners.owner: The Ffrench Revolution & Mrs T Hamilton

runners.name: Juan Les Pins
runners.spotlight: He had dropped a long way in the weights prior to popping up at Newcastle (5f) last month; ran well at Doncaster ten days later and back on the scoresheet with a Thirsk win last week (6f, good); won that apprentice race (no penalty today) last time with more authority than the winning margin might suggest but he is up in class today.
runners.comment: Unpenalised for last week's Thirsk win (6f, good) but this is a big step up in class

runners.name: Wheels Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Gelded after his 3yo campaign; down the field at Newcastle on return but much more like it when skipping clear in the closing stages at Musselburgh 13 days ago (5f, soft; edged left under pressure); return to 6f no problem but an 8lb rise in the weights might be.
runners.comment: Easy 5f win two weeks ago (soft ground); 8lb rise demands another personal best

runners.name: Photosynthesis
runners.spotlight: Disappointing at Newcastle in January; had earlier dug deep to win a Newmarket handicap (6f, good) on his final 4yo outing; third in this race 12 months ago and can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Third in this race last year; needs to bounce back from a poor run at Newcastle in January

runners.name: Addison Grey
runners.spotlight: Managed only a midfield finish in the Portland Handicap (5.6f, good) when last seen in September but he was favourite for that prestigious event having won well at Newmarket on penultimate start; still has potential after just six starts, three of which have resulted in 6f wins; looks the type to be contesting major sprint handicaps this summer but stall 1 is a slight query on his return (best pace looks middle to high); gelded since last seen.
runners.comment: 3-6 and open to further progress this year; gelded; stall 1 is perhaps not ideal today

runners.name: Arctic Thunder
runners.spotlight: String of runs last summer (6f-7f) suggested he was ahead of his mark but he often ran into trouble and/or got going too late; no surprise at all were he to take this back from a break but he is not one for the faint of heart.
runners.comment: Has lots of ability but often found trouble in running last year; can't be ignored

runners.name: Gweedore
runners.spotlight: He has been a fine advertisement for Katie Scott, winning 13 races over 7f and 1m; 0-6 at this trip but he was beaten less than one length in the 2023 Ayr Gold Cup; recent Musselburgh return should have blown away the cobwebs but others are still more appealing for win purposes.
runners.comment: Grand old servant but this is not sure to be the day he registers his first 6f success

runners.name: Hucklesbrook
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of useful 6f 3yo handicaps in the first half of 2025; no progress since but he is edging back down the weights; returns from four months off and he will need to be at his best.
runners.comment: Won two good 6f handicaps last summer; quiet since; not solid back from four months off

races.race_name: Slater Menswear Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Castle Ivers
runners.spotlight: Point and dual bumper winner who was a shade of odds-on when leading late on in a big-field maiden at Warwick in November 2024 (good to soft; won on soft); has a long absence to overcome and a hefty weight for his handicap debut, but trainer has spoken well of him.
runners.comment: Has a long absence to overcome and a hefty weight for his handicap debut

runners.name: Bibe Mus
runners.spotlight: 1m2f French Flat winner who did well for former Irish connections, in victory and defeat over hurdles; even better when winning comfortably on his stable debut at Sandown (soft) and it was only three days later he finished well held under a penalty (same mark here) in the Fred Winter, though that run was better than it looks on paper; this is his first handicap against older opposition and he's very much in the mix.
runners.comment: Debut winner for this yard and his Fred Winter run was better that it looks on paper

runners.name: Caballo De Guerra
runners.spotlight: Kelso novice winner in October (2m, good; effective on soft) and continued to run well until racing too freely in a Grade 2 at Kelso last time out; well held in a bumper when he tried a hood once before but this aid is worth another go.
runners.comment: Raced too freely in a Grade 2 last time; the ability is there should the hood help
runners.owner: A H B Hodge & Mrs H Cheape

runners.name: Tuppence
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden who took too strong a hold when a fading fourth on handicap debut at Musselburgh four weeks ago (good to soft); dropping back to 2m from 2m4f could work but this still commands a career best and by some way.
runners.comment: Dropping back to 2m could work but this still commands a career best and by some way

runners.name: Lance Les Des
runners.spotlight: Closely matched with Triple Crown Ted on February's Kelso clash and this 7yo was good value for his win back in a maiden here last time on soft ground; progressive and quite likely on fair terms with the handicapper.
runners.comment: Maiden winner last time; progressive and quite likely on fair terms with the handicapper

runners.name: Triple Crown Ted
runners.spotlight: Beat Lance Les Des and Bertie's Ballet off 4lb lower at Kelso in February (2m, heavy) and he was confidently ridden out the back when second over C&D last time (soft), where a late mistake didn't help; strong suspicion that he more to offer and the yard, also represented by Caballo De Guerra, won this with a novice two years ago.
runners.comment: Confidently ridden when second here last time; looks capable of better again

runners.name: Ginger Mail
runners.spotlight: Exposed 10yo whose powers would appear to be on the wane judged on this season's performances; opposable.
runners.comment: Exposed 10yo whose powers would appear to be on the wane
runners.owner: Mrs David Coltman & Mrs J Douglas Miller

runners.name: Bertie's Ballet
runners.spotlight: Won a Class 3 handicap at Haydock last season off this mark so wouldn't be too dismissive about his chances despite failing to reach anything like the same level in two of his three races this campaign.
runners.comment: Below par in two of his three races this season but he's on his last winning mark

runners.name: Belaya River
runners.spotlight: Flat background; mildly progressive when first then second in two 4yo hurdles but he ran miserably switched to handicaps at Sedgefield where his jumping didn't stand the test; returning to slower ground may help but unlikely that he's on that great a mark.
runners.comment: Returning to slower ground may help but unlikely that he's on that great a mark

runners.name: Ravenscraig Castle
runners.spotlight: Sole success in 17 runs over hurdles came at Perth last summer (2m, good); he's consistent but not progressive and was a well-held fifth in this last year off the very same mark.
runners.comment: Consistent but not progressive; fifth in this last year off the very same mark
runners.owner: A Sinclair, I Jardine & V Renwick

runners.name: The Gray Ghost
runners.spotlight: Versatile trip-wise as his recent run of solid form has been from 1m7f to 3m; should give his running but does look vulnerable off this high a mark in this strong a race.
runners.comment: In form but does look vulnerable off this high a mark in this strong a race

runners.name: Dance Thief
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been here, a hurdle and two chases; over fences for his last three races and has been good and bad; others preferred.
runners.comment: Over fences for his last three races and has been good and bad; others preferred

runners.name: Secret Force
runners.spotlight: On his first two runs for this yard he won a 1m3f handicap on heavy and a 3yo hurdle on good to yielding; not so good since, albeit at Grade 2 and Listed level; might yet do better again and canny Irish trainer fits two new forms of headgear for this handicap debut; interesting.
runners.comment: Highly tried since winning his 3yo hurdle; new headgear for this handicap debut

runners.name: Magna Victor
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo who is 2-2 since sent into handicaps, both wins over 1m7f on good to soft ground at Musselburgh; an 8lb rise seems harsh enough for last time, for all that he won readily, and this a lot deeper; still respected with him on an upward curve.
runners.comment: Up 8lb for winning a weaker race last time but he does seem on an upward curve

runners.name: Newtown Rambler
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner in Ireland and later placed in handicaps over fences; only his second run for this yard when dropped back to 2m in this new headgear combination at Warwick (good to soft) and he made pretty much every yard; it will be tougher dominating this stronger field on this more galloping track, and he's 6lb higher.
runners.comment: Made all at Warwick; it's tougher dominating around here and he's 6lb higher

races.race_name: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Koukeo
runners.spotlight: Won his first two chase starts in January (2m, good/good to soft) under Jonjo O'Neill, who is back on board today; disappointing at Newbury on his last two outings but the first was a tough assignment over 2m4f and he jumped badly last time; it wouldn't be a surprise to see him bounce back.
runners.comment: Disappointing on last 2 runs but good start to chasing career previously; may bounce back

runners.name: Pleasington
runners.spotlight: Two-time novice hurdle winner who ran well in defeat this term on two of his first three chase starts (1m7f/2m, soft/good) and is on a handy mark on that form; he's struggled on his last two runs and has something to prove but perhaps the wind op and first-time cheekpieces will make a difference.
runners.comment: He's struggled on his last two runs but perhaps wind op/cheekpieces will make a difference

runners.name: Throatlash
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; made mistakes and was under pressure when falling three out at Haydock (2m4f, soft) two months ago; however, he was 3-3 in handicap chases (1m7f-2m4f, soft/good to soft) previously and this very lightly raced 7yo may still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Front-runner; under pressure when falling last time but 3-3 in handicap chases previously

runners.name: Marty McFly
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who made it 4-4 for 2026 (1m7f-2m4f, good to soft/good) at Carlisle last month; won by 3l last time and a 7lb rise may not stop this thriving 8yo, provided the forecast soft ground isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Arrives on a five-timer and latest 7lb rise may not stop this thriving 8yo

runners.name: Ballyfort
runners.spotlight: Made it two wins for the season when scoring over C&D (soft) in January and bounced back from lesser run here when 1l second of five over C&D (soft) last month; could give another good account but he needs to be better than ever back up in grade if he's to win today.
runners.comment: Likes it here & has won twice this term but needs to be better than ever back up in grade

runners.name: Tanking Along
runners.spotlight: Progressive in cheekpieces, winning at Sedgefield in November (2m1f, soft; hurdles) and March (2m, good; fences); should appreciate going back left-handed after being soundly beaten at Carlisle last time but he's 8lb out of the handicap today and the cheekpieces are left off.
runners.comment: Two wins at Sedgefield this season but 8lb wrong today and the cheekpieces are left off

runners.name: The Grafter
runners.spotlight: Built upon his hurdling promise to win on chase/seasonal debut at Newcastle (2m, heavy) in January and he followed up there (2m, good to soft) in February; climbing the weights but this unexposed 7yo had something to spare for both wins and may well have more left in the tank.
runners.comment: 2-2 over fences and something to spare each time; may well have more left in the tank

runners.name: Risk De Pluie
runners.spotlight: Beaten on first seven chase starts but showed improved form to win comfortably at Chepstow (2m3f, soft) in February and he bumped into a progressive winner there (2m3f, soft) last month, when clear of the third; there can be optimism that he will be fine back down in trip and he's respected.
runners.comment: In good form at Chepstow on last two starts; optimism he'll be fine back down in trip

runners.name: Stuntman Steve
runners.spotlight: Hard to fancy on the evidence of his four runs over fences this year; however, he's well handicapped on last summer's hurdle form and he's from a respected Irish stable; one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on chasing evidence but this Irish raider is well treated on his hurdle form

races.race_name: Abbott Risk Consulting Ltd Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dropematthestation
runners.spotlight: Has been running well for a long way before weakening in maidens over 2m3f and 2m5f (tongue tied in final run); could have a better run in him after wind surgery and he's of definite interest.
runners.comment: Has been weakening over longer trips in previous maidens; had a wind operation

runners.name: Lirion
runners.spotlight: Bought for £60,000 after finishing an 11l second to the classy Mydaddypaddy in a Huntingdon bumper last March; only returned this February when beaten a short-head here by a Russell/Scudamore horse with potential; now goes over hurdles.
runners.comment: Two encouraging runs in bumpers, the latest after a long absence; hurdle debut
runners.owner: Mrs Diana L Whateley, McNeill & Stone Families

runners.name: Green Angel
runners.spotlight: A good run to be fourth on yielding ground in a 2m1f maiden at Down Royal in December and when below that level here two months ago he was sent over 2m4f (on soft); connections presumably not fazed by today's ground and, returned to shorter, he shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: 2m4f perhaps too far last time; previous Down Royal fourth over 2m1f was a good effort

runners.name: Linalene
runners.spotlight: By some way it was his best performance yet when the only one to give the odds-on winner a race over C&D (soft) in late March; may need to take another step forward here but that's quite feasible.
runners.comment: By some way it was his best performance yet when second to odds-on winner here last time

runners.name: Penn Avenue
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner; 0-6 over hurdles and the worst of those runs was the latest in a handicap at Carlisle; gets all the allowances as a 4yo filly but others probably have greater upside even if she is back to something like her best.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles; gets all the allowances as a 4yo filly but others have greater upside

runners.name: Bohemond Antioch
runners.spotlight: 0-3 in bumpers and 0-4 over hurdles, but has some respectable form and wasn't himself at Sedgefield last time when held at his point of departure; had a break since Christmas and can go well if back on song.
runners.comment: Below par at Christmas but had a break and can go well if back on song

runners.name: Culzean
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in bumpers or over hurdles and she can be readily dismissed.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form in bumpers or over hurdles and she can be readily dismissed

runners.name: Magna Patch
runners.spotlight: Close second in point bumper but it's been a quiet start under rules with 286 days separating heavy defeats in a bumper and novice hurdle.
runners.comment: Close second in point bumper but it's been a quiet start under rules

races.race_name: Hillhouse Quarry Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Il Ridoto
runners.spotlight: 9yo who hasn't been at his best this season but there have been signs of spark and he failed to stay 3m2f when well beaten at the Cheltenham Festival last time; continues to drop down the weights and could be in the shake-up back down in trip and in grade.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark and could be in the shake-up back down in trip and in grade

runners.name: Jipcot
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in the Plate last month but Cheltenham may not be his track; comfortably made all in small field at Leicester (2m6f, good to soft) in January and led into the straight at Ascot (3m, good to soft) in February, only to weaken; also effective on soft; could be involved.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Cheltenham last month but that may not be his track; in good heart before

runners.name: Califet En Vol
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten fourth at Kempton (2m4f, good) last month but mistakes didn't help; won there (2m4f, good to soft) two starts ago and he can be in the mix if cutting out the errors; probably okay on soft ground.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton in February and mistakes didn't help when soundly beaten there last time

runners.name: Twinjets
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; won two in a row last autumn (2m3f/2m4f, good to soft) and back to form when second under Jay Tidball at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) last month; effective on soft earlier in career; can make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Front-runner who was second at Newbury last month and can make another bold bid

runners.name: Myretown
runners.spotlight: Prone to making a bad blunder and arrives with something to prove following heavy defeats on his last two starts; however, he hasn't been devoid of promise this season and he'll be a major player if tapping back into the promise shown when romping to victory at last season's Cheltenham Festival.
runners.comment: Has something to prove but major player if tapping back into last season's promise

runners.name: Donnacha
runners.spotlight: Can make mistakes but he was runner-up twice in a row prior to winning a quality handicap at Cheltenham (2m4f, soft) in January, when clear with subsequent Ultima runner-up Jagwar; a 5lb rise for that career-best performance isn't harsh and he's entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Won a quality handicap at Cheltenham in January and a 5lb rise isn't harsh

runners.name: Hombre De Guerra
runners.spotlight: Hasn't got competitive this term and beaten 18l when sixth of eight on stable debut at Kelso (2m1f, good to soft) in February; however, he shaped as though this step up in trip might be a good move and his three wins, including two over fences last January (2m, soft), have all come at Ayr.
runners.comment: Yet to get competitive this season but his three wins have all come at Ayr

runners.name: Hoe Joly Smoke
runners.spotlight: He hasn't quite been at the top of his game on his last two starts; however, he's had wind surgery since and is certainly of interest on his two third-place finishes at Cheltenham in the autumn (3m1f/2m4f, good/soft); top trainer has won five of the last eight runnings of this race.
runners.comment: Has had wind surgery and top trainer has won five of the last eight runnings of this race

runners.name: Dubai Days
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (soft) in February before respectable sixth of 17 in veterans' final at Haydock (2m4f, good) a fortnight ago; 11 visits to Ayr have yielded five wins and this is his time of year; could go well.
runners.comment: 11 visits to Ayr have yielded five wins and this is his time of year; could go well

races.race_name: Coral Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Came From Nowhere
runners.spotlight: Improved in this tongue-tie in the winter, winning two handicaps (2m3f, soft), and he may well have completed a hat-trick but for a final-flight mistake in a novice at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) four weeks ago; this smooth-travelling sort could have more left in the tank; strong claims.
runners.comment: May well have completed hat-trick but for final-flight mistake at Newbury; strong claims

runners.name: Eagles Reprieve
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who got off the mark over hurdles at the fourth attempt at Kelso (2m5f, soft) in November and was back on the scoresheet in a novice there (2m5f, good to soft) four weeks ago; did it easily last time and has potential off his opening mark.
runners.comment: Maiden/novice wins at Kelso; did it easily last time and has potential off opening mark

runners.name: Spectacularsunrise
runners.spotlight: Merely mid-division in the Morebattle at Kelso (2m, good to soft) in February having been backed into favouritism; however, he's been a promising novice this season and is capable of better than he showed last time; not written off up in trip.
runners.comment: Promising novice this season and better than he showed in the Morebattle; not written off

runners.name: Knomorediamonds
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Hexham (2m4f, good to soft) last October and he's continued to run well (soft/good to soft), finishing 11l fourth of 17 at Haydock (2m5f) in January on his last handicap start; not ruled out each-way although there's a suspicion he's vulnerable to stronger stayers at this trip.
runners.comment: In good form this season but suspicion he's vulnerable to stronger stayers at this trip

runners.name: A Perfect Day
runners.spotlight: Has done well since moving into handicaps, winning at Lingfield (2m7f, good to soft) in December and second of 14 at Uttoxeter (2m4f, soft) last month behind Wellington Arch, who followed up at Aintree last week; progressing nicely and he's a key player up 3lb for last time.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Uttoxeter last month and winner followed up at Aintree last week; key player

runners.name: The Four Sixes
runners.spotlight: Easily made all in this last year when 5lb higher and he's one to watch in the betting; however, he's struggled for form over fences/hurdles since winning a novice chase at Kelso in December.
runners.comment: Easily made all in this last year but he's struggled for form since chase win in December

runners.name: Serious Operator
runners.spotlight: 9yo who was tailed off at Kelso in February last time but that was the competitive Morebattle Hurdle over 2m; in good form over 2m4f at the end of last year, winning at Newcastle then second at Aintree; won this in 2023 on sole previous course visit and has an each-way shout back up in trip.
runners.comment: Won this in 2023 on sole previous course visit and has an each-way shout

runners.name: Captain Butler
runners.spotlight: Runner-up here (2m4f, soft) in November and suited by the step back up in trip when fourth of 14 at Kelso (2m5f, good to soft) four weeks ago, beaten just over 2l; that form looks solid; could again be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Fourth of 14 at Kelso last time and the form looks solid; could again be thereabouts

runners.name: Jony R
runners.spotlight: Improved form since this hood went on, comfortably defying a penalty in novice hurdle at Down Royal (2m1f, soft) in January before respectable efforts in 2m Listed/Grade 3 races; however, further improvement is needed back in a handicap and he has to prove his stamina.
runners.comment: Improved form in this hood; contested Grade 3 last time; more needed back in a handicap

runners.name: Sanilam
runners.spotlight: Won maiden hurdle here (2m, soft) in January and back on the scoresheet in this hood on his last two starts, most recently on handicap debut at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft); this progressive 6yo did it nicely last time and a 5lb rise may not stop him this afternoon.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 in this hood when winning on h'cap debut at Musselburgh; clearly progressive
runners.owner: J Fyffe And Mrs W Duffus

runners.name: Saracen Beau
runners.spotlight: Went back up in trip at Doncaster (2m5f, good to soft) last month on his second start since rejoining this yard and posted a ready win; this lightly raced 7yo could take a 7lb rise in his stride provided conditions are suitable (unproven on soft ground).
runners.comment: Ready win at Doncaster last month; could take 7lb rise in his stride; unproven on soft

runners.name: Minella Missile
runners.spotlight: This season hasn't been completely devoid of spark and he continues to drop down the weights, but he hasn't been shaping as though he's about to take advantage and his losing sequence is up to 11 races.
runners.comment: Continues to drop down weights but hasn't been shaping as though about to take advantage

runners.name: Phantomofthepoints
runners.spotlight: Fourth in this last year (good to soft) and he comfortably made all at Warwick (2m5f, heavy) in February; pulled up at Fontwell 15 days later but that was a Grade 2; could make a bold bid back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Won at Warwick in February and when pulled up at Fontwell last time it was a Grade 2
runners.owner: Mrs Jo Tracey, K Alexander, P&S Bryceland

races.race_name: Get Home Safe With Thistle Cabs Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Woodland Park
runners.spotlight: Admirably consistent in handicaps and finished second to an unexposed Skelton winner when upped in trip at Wetherby (3m, soft) in February, pulling clear of the third; solid claims up 3lb for last time.
runners.comment: Second to unexposed Skelton winner at Wetherby in February and has solid claims up 3lb

runners.name: Hidden History
runners.spotlight: Below par on last three starts, most recently at Uttoxeter last month following wind surgery; however, he won off this mark on his reappearance at Aintree in November and first-time cheekpieces might give him a boost; in top hands and not written off.
runners.comment: A return to form is needed but cheekpieces go on and he's in top hands; not written off

runners.name: Don Rafael
runners.spotlight: He's done well since joining this yard and was having his first crack at a staying trip when winning over 3m1f at Hereford last month; did it only narrowly and is 3lb higher in a hotter race today, but he might have more to offer over this sort of distance.
runners.comment: Up in grade after narrow Hereford win but he's unexposed at this sort of distance

runners.name: Draco Malfoy
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in mid-division on last month's handicap debut in the EBF Final at Sandown (2m4f, soft); however, that was a hot contest and this two-time novice hurdle winner (2m4f/2m5f, soft) retains potential, with the step up in trip a possible plus.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in the EBF Final on handicap debut but this 6yo retains potential
runners.owner: Sir F Brooke & Sir R Pilkington

runners.name: Whistle Stop Tour
runners.spotlight: Well beaten/pulled up over fences on his last two starts but he's produced positive performances over C&D (soft) on his two hurdle runs this season, finishing placed each time; each-way claims back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Hasn't shone over fences on last two runs but each-way claims on this season's hurdle form
runners.owner: Mrs Jo Tracey, K Alexander & Russell

runners.name: Society Soldier
runners.spotlight: Placed in a 3m point and she got off the mark at the third attempt over hurdles in a mares' novice here (2m4f, soft) last month; did it nicely from a solid yardstick and she's from the family of Paisley Park, so could appreciate the step up in trip on handicap debut; possible player.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced mare who could appreciate the step up in trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Alentejo
runners.spotlight: Three-time French hurdle winner who was pulled up on his chase and stable debut in January but returned to form when second over hurdles at Newbury (3m, heavy) in February; that latest form has worked out well and he retains the same mark; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Ex-French 7yo; the form of his Newbury second has worked out well; firmly in calculations
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs J D Cotton

runners.name: I Am Max
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent this season and down the field at Haydock a fortnight ago; however, he ran pretty well when runner-up at Doncaster two starts ago and is 2lb lower than when a close fourth of 13 in this race last year; if he's on song in a first-time tongue-tie he might not be far away.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this term but ran pretty well two starts ago, and also in this race last year

runners.name: Hawthorn Street
runners.spotlight: Point winner who got off the mark over hurdles at the third attempt at Catterick (3m1f, soft) in February and was beaten only 4l when fourth on last month's handicap debut at Wetherby (3m, good to soft); unexposed and further improvement is possible.
runners.comment: Ran well in fourth on handicap debut at Wetherby and further improvement is possible
runners.owner: Toby Cole & Stars Of The Box

runners.name: Irish Champ
runners.spotlight: 3m point winner who showed promise in novice/maiden hurdles here earlier in the year (2m/2m3f, soft) and stayed on strongly when 13l seventh of 19 on handicap debut at Down Royal (2m4f, yielding) last month; remains lightly raced and this step up in trip could be right up his street.
runners.comment: Stayed on strongly over 2m4f on handicap debut and this trip could be right up his street

runners.name: Billy Boi Blue
runners.spotlight: Tailed off (fences) and pulled up (hurdles) on his last two starts; a major turnaround is necessary but he runs off the same mark as when winning this race last year; not ruled out and the betting may be interesting.
runners.comment: He's struggled on his last two starts but won this last year; betting may be interesting

runners.name: Rocheval
runners.spotlight: 0-8 since winning his first three starts in the 2023-24 season; still only six and he was a fairly encouraging fourth of 13 over C&D in January last time, but needs to build on that run if he's to threaten today.
runners.comment: Fairly encouraging fourth over C&D in January last time but needs to build on that today

runners.name: Shoeshine Boy
runners.spotlight: Course specialist who won two in a row over C&D (heavy/soft) in January; he's better than he showed when 15l third over C&D (soft) in February and the subsequent break may have helped, but this 10yo may still be vulnerable back up in grade.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins in January but this 10yo may be vulnerable back up in grade

runners.name: Kent De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Easily won a maiden hurdle at Carlisle (2m3f, soft) in December and he tapped back into that promise to win a handicap at Wetherby (3m, good) three weeks ago on his stable debut; beat just five rivals last time but this 6yo is unexposed over staying trips and could be a contender up 4lb.
runners.comment: Won on stable debut at Wetherby; tougher task today but unexposed over staying trips

runners.name: Eyed
runners.spotlight: His chase form has been mixed this season but he faced a tough task from out of the handicap at the Cheltenham Festival (pulled up); runner-up at Kempton (2m5f) in November on last hurdle start and he was second over fences at this meeting 12 months ago (3m); might not be a forlorn hope today.
runners.comment: Tough task when pulled up at Cheltenham Festival; ran well at this meeting last year

runners.name: Just Ennemi
runners.spotlight: 2-3 over hurdles in France; he's returned from a long absence this season and been well beaten in a chase and handicap hurdle; hopes are pinned on the wind op and first-time tongue-tie prompting a transformation.
runners.comment: Hopes are pinned on the wind op and first-time tongue-tie prompting a transformation

runners.name: Patriotik
runners.spotlight: Very well handicapped on staying form as recent as last March and it's possible the step back up in trip will be a positive; however, unable to pose a threat across his six runs this season and has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on form as recent as last March but unable to pose a threat this season

races.race_name: Jardine Utilities Race To Repair 'Hands & Heels' Handicap Hurdle (Conditional/Amateurs') (RE Series)

runners.name: Kap Boy
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper for Paul Nicholls and added a 2m4f handicap at Perth in October (soft) on his first run for Olly Murphy; ran well afterwards over 2m5f, and 3m was perhaps too far when last seen three months ago; he could be handicapped on the high side but capable of having some say.
runners.comment: Perhaps handicapped on the high side but capable of having some say after a break

runners.name: Paso Doble
runners.spotlight: His early-season form (2m3f-2m7f) offers encouragement off this mark; last two runs well below his best, though heavy ground was a feasible excuse the last time when a tongue-tie joined his regular cheekpieces; hard to rule out.
runners.comment: His early-season form (2m3f-2m7f) offers encouragement off this mark

runners.name: Balboa
runners.spotlight: Without a win since 2023 but he has dropped to a dangerous mark and ran surprisingly well over 3m last time at Wincanton when all his form is over shorter; goes on soft.
runners.comment: Ran surprisingly well over 3m last time at Wincanton when all his form is over shorter

runners.name: Guchen
runners.spotlight: Ridden by Aamilah Aswat in his last five races; he's won the last two, though a 19l margin of victory at Newbury last time (2m4f, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie) provided the handicapper with the ammunition to hit him with a 10lb hike; he still rates a likely contender as a 6yo firmly on the up.
runners.comment: Hit with a 10lb hike for latest 19l win at Newbury, but he's a 6yo firmly on the up

runners.name: Nice One Eric
runners.spotlight: Had them well strung out in a 2m Southwell maiden (soft) in early March but that was a modest contest, for all that the second and third horses have won since, in low-grade races on the Flat and over hurdles; this 5yo may need to be on a sharp upward curve if he's to defy this initial mark.
runners.comment: This 5yo may need to be on a sharp upward curve if he's to defy this initial mark

runners.name: Thankyouandplease
runners.spotlight: Goes on all ground; unseated four out when favourite at Wincanton last time; had been in career-best form with a win at Wincanton (1m7f) and a second at Taunton (2m3f); even more needed here, however.
runners.comment: Unseated four out last time at Wincanton and in career-best form prior to that

runners.name: Summergrounds
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of this 12 months ago when 10lb lower; has shown that he can deal with this high a mark and he goes well fresh, so a 75-day break after being below par last time is probably a good thing; respected.
runners.comment: Last year's winner who goes well fresh, so a 75-day break is probably a good thing

runners.name: Travis Wheatley
runners.spotlight: The first-time cheekpieces appeared to make a huge difference at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) where he jumped well in front and won unchallenged by just over 5l; 9lb higher in a higher grade but he arrives here a relative unknown quantity, especially over this far.
runners.comment: In the new cheekpieces he jumped well and won unchallenged at Newcastle; up 9lb

runners.name: Rickety Gate
runners.spotlight: Exposed five-time winner who ran perfectly well back from an absence at Carlisle but it's never ideal being 5lb out of the weights in this competitive a handicap.
runners.comment: It's never ideal being 5lb out of the weights in this competitive a handicap

races.race_name: Country And Western Raceday - 28th June Handicap

runners.name: Travel Agent
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who won at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack) for Martin Dunne last summer and he got back near that level when a close second at Kempton (eyeshield added) last week; that was only his second run for Adam Kirby and he should get a good tow into this race; interesting contender off same mark as last time.
runners.comment: Close second at Kempton last week and he's a key player off same mark here

runners.name: Style King
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough with a front-running win at Lingfield (6f, AW) in September but he's been disappointing since; may have needed his reappearance run last month but he's now 1-14 and this looks tough from a wide draw.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield in the autumn but he's been disappointing since and others are preferred

runners.name: Good Karma
runners.spotlight: Went close here (7f) on his penultimate run but he's not easy to predict and was well held when favourite at Thirsk (7f, good) last Friday; needs to step up again after that and all of his three wins have been over 7f; first-time visor.
runners.comment: Disappointing when favourite at Thirsk latest and all of his three wins have been over 7f

runners.name: White Umbrella
runners.spotlight: Her last four wins have been at this track and latest was off 1lb lower over C&D last June; mixed results in three runs this year but she had traffic issues last time and was placed here (6f) on her penultimate run; in the mix.
runners.comment: Her last four wins have been at this track and she was placed over C&D on penultimate run

runners.name: Jojo Rabbit
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when making all off a reduced mark at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) ten days ago; remains feasibly treated under a penalty and his four runs over this trip include a creditable third over C&D; respected.
runners.comment: Made all at Pontefract and he's respected under a penalty on this step back up in trip
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing XIII & Ruth Carr

runners.name: Twilight Madness
runners.spotlight: Ten-time sprint winner who scored at Bath (5f, good) two weeks ago and the quick turnaround may have caused his downfall at Kempton three days later; has claims if he can recapture that penultimate form and his four runs over C&D include a win and two thirds; dangerous.
runners.comment: Won at Bath on his penultimate run and is a big player if he can recapture that form
runners.owner: P R Hedger & P C F Racing Ltd

runners.name: Megaphone
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW early last year but he's been regressive since and has finished ahead of only one rival in four runs for his current yard; mark continues to fall but he's now 0-13 in handicaps and slow starts have been a recurring theme; lots to prove.
runners.comment: Regressive 4yo who has finished ahead of only one rival in four runs for his current yard

runners.name: Due Date
runners.spotlight: Four wins in a purple patch in the second half of 2025; now 2lb lower than for his last success but he's struggled at big prices in four runs this year and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: On handy mark but he's been out of sorts this year and needs to get back on track
runners.owner: A Bangs & Miss J A Challen

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Afton Down
runners.spotlight: Promising debut here last September where he was green and looked stretched by 7f, losing four places in the final 110 yards; found improvement dropped to 6f when runner-up at Brighton the following month and he ended last season with another close call at Chelmsford in October; leading form contender on his return and he should have more to offer as a 3yo.
runners.comment: Ended last season with two close seconds and he's a key player on his return

runners.name: Cool Doc Boy
runners.spotlight: Market springer when runner-up on Kempton debut (6f, Polytrack) in November; failed to build on that over C&D five days later but he had a tough draw and that may have come too soon; looks interesting on his initial form and he's respected back from a break.
runners.comment: Looks interesting on his debut second at Kempton and he's respected back from a break

runners.name: Goldenstateofmind
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start when runner-up at Thirsk (5f, good) last May but he was well held when upped to 6f at Chester four weeks later; gelded since and has something to prove after 11 months off.
runners.comment: Disappointing in his second 2yo run and has something to prove after 11 months off

runners.name: Scheffler
runners.spotlight: 62,000euros yearling; 11-1 with blinkers applied on Lingfield debut (7f, Polytrack) last month but he didn't get much luck before finishing well for third; that was eye-catching and he's a big player if he can build on that on this drop in trip.
runners.comment: Eye-catcher on recent Lingfield debut (7f) and he's a big player if he can build on that

runners.name: Hamda's Joy
runners.spotlight: Finished third in two AW runs in the autumn but she was beaten 5l when odds-on at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) last time; returns after 195 days off and she needs improvement to make a major impact here.
runners.comment: Placed in two AW runs in the autumn but she was beaten 5l when odds-on last time

runners.name: Mayflower Rock
runners.spotlight: 7,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Bake (7f/1m; RPR 89) and Doddie's Impact (5f including 2yo/AW; 72); dam unraced half-sister to three winners including Meringue Pie (6f 2yo) and Quoteline Direct (6f-1m2f); yard 10% in novice events and market should guide on debut
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners at up to 1m and market should guide on debut

runners.name: Sargent Dennis
runners.spotlight: 18-1 on Chelmsford debut (6f, AW) last month and he never got involved before finishing a 9l fifth of 11; didn't get the best of runs but he has plenty find here and could be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Shaped with some promise on Kempton debut last month but he has plenty to find here

runners.name: Hallandale Beach
runners.spotlight: 55,000gns yearling; Havana Grey half-brother to five winners; weak 9-1 shot at Chelmsford (6f, AW) last month but he ran well for a long way before finishing fourth behind an odds-on rival; was beaten 7l but he should benefit from that experience and is open to progress on his second start.
runners.comment: Ran well for long way when fourth at Chelmsford and he should benefit from that experience

runners.name: Kiani King
runners.spotlight: Faded into a well-held fourth of six on C&D debut last month and he recorded an RPR of 53 in that race; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well-held fourth of six on C&D debut last month and he recorded an RPR of 53
runners.owner: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster

races.race_name: Get The Inside Track With raceday-ready.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Robusto
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 18 and has to put a poor recent effort at Lingfield (1m4f, Polytrack) behind him, but he often makes the running and could enjoy the run of the race if again ridden positively; visor removed after one use.
runners.comment: Ran poorly last time, but often makes the running and could enjoy the run of the race

runners.name: Solar Pass
runners.spotlight: Unexposed filly who has proved consistent on the AW (721421), doubling her tally with a game success at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta) last month after 182 days off (third and fourth won next time); only 3lb higher and has run well over this far on turf; major player.
runners.comment: Winning return at Southwell last month; has run well over this far on turf; major player

runners.name: Diamond Bay
runners.spotlight: Two wins here including over this trip, but he ran very poorly on his return from four months off at Lingfield (2m, Polytrack) a fortnight ago, especially as he proven fresh; visor returns.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but he ran very poorly on his Lingfield return a fortnight ago

runners.name: Caramay
runners.spotlight: Tough mare who has been in good form since December (1334133), the wins coming at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta) and Kempton (1m3f, Polytrack); has never attempted this far before, but still respected.
runners.comment: In good form since December; has never attempted this far before, but still respected

runners.name: Baileys Khelstar
runners.spotlight: Three wins including an emphatic success at Southwell (1m6f, Tapeta) on his 4yo reappearance, but he has plenty to prove having been beaten a very long way on his return from almost 21 months off at Musselburgh (1m6f, soft) 13 days ago.
runners.comment: Beaten a very long way on his return from almost 21 months off at Musselburgh 13 days ago
runners.owner: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)

runners.name: Crackergee
runners.spotlight: Has ability but has also proved wildly inconsistent and is now 0-9; probably best watched with tongue-tie removed on return from 130 days off.
runners.comment: Wildly inconsistent and is now 0-9; probably best watched on return from 130 days off

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Alondra
runners.spotlight: Record over C&D in February read 112, but she seemed to be beaten fair and square back here last month; the handicapper may have her for the time being.
runners.comment: Record over C&D in February read 112, but seemed to be beaten fair and square last time

runners.name: Gold Star Hero
runners.spotlight: Record on the AW reads 1211 and he was impressive when making virtually all over 6f here eight days ago; 5lb penalty and return to 5f shouldn't preclude another big performance.
runners.comment: Record on AW reads 1211; 5lb penalty and return to 5f shouldn't stop another big effort

runners.name: Accrual
runners.spotlight: Two wins and two runner-up finishes from five visits here, but not at his best in his recent completed starts; others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Two wins and two seconds from five visits here, but not at his best in recent starts

runners.name: Havana Blast
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced gelding whose sole win came at Chelmsford (5f, Polytrack; first-time tongue-tie) in August 2024; was entitled to need the run after 19 months off at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta) three weeks ago and a hood is now given a go, but others looks more solid.
runners.comment: Entitled to need the run at Newcastle three weeks ago, others looks more solid; hood on
