runners.name: Counsel
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-2 over C&D when scraping home last month; up 2lb, but that latest run came after 79 days off so he could be a bit sharper for it; high on list.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 over C&D when scraping home last month; up 2lb, but still high on list

runners.name: Ziggy's Missile
runners.spotlight: Not seen in six months, but finished first and second in two starts over C&D as a 3yo, on each occasion when returning from an absence; 6lb below last winning mark; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Proven fresh and has finished first and second in two starts over C&D; worth a second look

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Grow Old With Me
runners.spotlight: 260,000gns yearling; surrounded by subsequent winners when beaten a neck into second of 15 on his Newmarket debut (7f, good; 11-4) in November 2024; related to AW winners and is a fascinating contender on return from 18 months off, especially if the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck on his Newmarket debut in November 2024; fascinating on return

runners.name: Mr Writer
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in three outings on turf last year, but finished runner-up in both starts on the Kempton Polytrack (6f) albeit the latest was in a three-runner contest; may find a few too good on return.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both starts on Polytrack last year, but may find a few too good on return

runners.name: Law Court
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling; showed plenty of ability when making the frame in both starts at Newbury (7f/6.5f) last August; needs this for a mark, but still a player on form and is closely related to an AW winner.
runners.comment: In the frame in both starts on turf last August; closely related to an AW winner; player

runners.name: Ahead Of Fashion
runners.spotlight: 0-9 albeit placed three times including on Polytrack; well beaten in three handicaps, though, and disappointing for the others if he proves good enough.
runners.comment: 0-9; disappointing for the others if he proves good enough

runners.name: Anton Mauve
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns foal, 250,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Unanimous Consent (US 1m/8.5f turf including 2yo/stakes; RPR 109), Star Music (7f AW 2yo; 94) and Golden Passport (7f/1m including 2yo/AW; 82); dam maiden (76) half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner; already gelded, but makes plenty of appeal on breeding and market support would be significant.
runners.comment: Makes plenty of appeal on breeding and market support would be significant on debut

runners.name: Beautiful Rumour
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 84), sister to 5f winner Dancing Juice; probably best watched on belated debut unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Probably best watched on belated debut unless the market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Slight Of Foot
runners.spotlight: Finished well behind Law Court when last of ten on his Newbury debut (7f, good to firm) last August, but much better when second of 14 at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) the following month; off seven months since and has been gelded, but still high on list if taking another step forward.
runners.comment: Improved from debut when second at Kempton in September; high on list if progressing again

runners.name: Korbut
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival on her debut here (8.6f) last month, but showed more when second of eight at Southwell (7f, Tapeta; 33-1) ten days ago; that was a steadily run affair, though, so not sure how strong the form is.
runners.comment: Second in a steadily run affair at Southwell ten days ago; not sure how strong the form is

runners.name: Battle Standard
runners.spotlight: Comfortably held in two starts at Southwell (1m/7f) last month; handicaps become an option after this.
runners.comment: Comfortably held in two starts at Southwell last month; handicaps an option after this
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Luansobe
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when second of nine on his Kempton debut (7f, Polytrack) in October, but that race was slowly run and he only beat one home at Newmarket (7f, good) later in month; bit to prove on return.
runners.comment: Runner-up on Kempton debut in October, but well held at Newmarket next time; bit to prove

races.race_name: Download The Racecourse App Raceday Ready Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Tryst
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when landing a novice over C&D in October, but a bit disappointing when only fourth of six having been sent off odds-on for last month's Kempton return (1m, Polytrack); perhaps the run was just needed and the return here is a positive on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Disappointing on her Kempton return, but won over C&D in October; return here a positive

runners.name: Politely
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two turf runs last summer, but a complete contrast when bolting up by 5l in a Lingfield maiden (1m, Polytrack) four weeks ago (runner-up has won since); this is likely to be harder on handicap debut, but she is clearly progressive.
runners.comment: Bolted up by 5l in a Lingfield maiden four weeks ago; runner-up has won since; progressive

runners.name: Just A Girl
runners.spotlight: Bolted up from the front in a Lingfield novice (7.6f, good) last August; ran with credit when third of eight on her return from five months off at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) 12 days ago, but probably needs more back up in trip.
runners.comment: Ran with credit when third on her Southwell return 12 days ago, but probably needs more

runners.name: Orangesandlemons
runners.spotlight: Consistent in maiden/novices on the AW at the start of her career (22221) and has since shown some good form in turf handicaps, including in headgear; off six months, but has gone well fresh before; considered.
runners.comment: Consistent on the AW at the start of her career (22221); can go well fresh; considered

runners.name: Maeva
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in handicaps last year including at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack); far from disgraced when sixth of 18 in a Saint-Cloud Listed event (1m, heavy) on her final outing and respected if ready to go after 159 days off.
runners.comment: Ended last year in good form and respected if ready to go after 159 days off

races.race_name: Race And Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap

runners.name: Naughty Niall
runners.spotlight: Can be relied upon to give his true running around here, as was the case last week when passed late on by Port Noir; solid chance.
runners.comment: Port Noir finished just the stronger here last week but this 5yo always goes well here

runners.name: Ignition
runners.spotlight: No wins in 17 but after racing over further he hit the frame over C&D 11 days ago; could nick a race sooner or later off this low a mark.
runners.comment: No wins in 17 but after racing over further he hit the frame over C&D 11 days ago

runners.name: Arlecchino's Rex
runners.spotlight: Beat Al Shabab over C&D in March and has continued to run well off this 3lb higher mark; considered.
runners.comment: Beat Al Shabab over C&D in March and has continued to run well off this 3lb higher mark

runners.name: Port Noir
runners.spotlight: Bang-in-form 9yo who loves it here and has no penalty for beating Naughty Niall last week; she was nicely on top in the finish.
runners.comment: In-form 9yo who loves it here and has no penalty for beating Naughty Niall last week

runners.name: Desiderata
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-21; arrives in form after a win here over 8.6f and a close third over 1m2f at Lingfield; 3lb higher than for those two runs but should be on the scene.
runners.comment: Arrives in form after a win here over 8.6f and a close third over 1m2f at Lingfield

runners.name: Wrist Art
runners.spotlight: 2-41 overall and 0-8 for current yard; close up on a couple of occasions this winter but backwards steps in his last two races (visored; tongue-tie/blinkers); risky enough.
runners.comment: Close up on a couple of occasions this winter but backwards steps in his last two races

runners.name: Bossy Parker
runners.spotlight: His third win here when holding on grimly last month (8.6f), ending a two-year losing streak; only 2lb higher but he wouldn't be an obvious one to follow up and has never raced over quite this far.
runners.comment: Snapped a two-year losing streak here last month; up 2lb but also up in trip

runners.name: Roman Secret
runners.spotlight: Only 1-21, the win coming over 8.6f here in late 2023 for Richard Fahey; off since October when she wasn't in great form.
runners.comment: Sole win was here in 2023; off since October when she wasn't in great form
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Balqaa
runners.spotlight: It was a classified race she won over C&D last month but this mark isn't beyond her and a slow start was no help at Southwell.
runners.comment: It was a classified race she won over C&D last month but this mark isn't beyond her

runners.name: Like Magic
runners.spotlight: Dual winner in Germany; has hurtled down the ratings in Britain for good reason but having first run for Mark Loughnane when showing more here 18 days ago (8.6f; 5-1); he faded late on after being up there; could be on the verge of turning a corner.
runners.comment: Dual winner in Germany; struggled here but could be on the verge of turning a corner

runners.name: Busby
runners.spotlight: Only 2l away over 1m4f here in early March and last win was over C&D just over a year ago; however, this veteran is usually opposable these days.
runners.comment: Multiple winner but this veteran is usually opposable these days

runners.name: Scenario
runners.spotlight: Handy throughout and won by 1.5l over 1m at Bath 12 days ago; no good thing to defy a penalty when she's anything but consistent and drawn in 12.
runners.comment: Bath winner; no good thing to defy a penalty when anything but consistent and drawn in 12

races.race_name: Blackmore Build And Design Handicap

runners.name: The Craftymaster
runners.spotlight: Got back on the scoresheet at Lingfield (2m, AW) last month and he ran to a similar level when fourth at Wolverhampton (1m6f) 11 days ago; on lower mark back on turf but he's struggled in his six runs in this sphere including three 2m handicaps; risks attached.
runners.comment: Nine-time AW winner but he's struggled in his six runs on turf; opposable
runners.owner: R Miles, V Miles, T Stamp & Partner

runners.name: Bashful Boy
runners.spotlight: Veteran who hasn't won since 2023 but he's been in fair form on AW this spring including when fourth on Polytrack (2m) in his last two starts; should be in the mix again but he needs to find more to make a big impact; cheekpieces are removed and blinkers return.
runners.comment: In fair form on AW this spring but he hasn't won since 2023; needs to find more

runners.name: Educator
runners.spotlight: Triple turf winner who finished well to go close off this mark at Kempton (2m, AW) in January; flopped with a heavy defeat at Newcastle the following month but that was a Class 3 and has claims if he can bounce back after a short break; has form on good to firm and good to soft.
runners.comment: Flopped at Newcastle latest but that was a Class 3 and has claims if he can bounce back

runners.name: Man Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Has done a lot of his winning in Jersey but he scored here (1m5f, firm) in the autumn and was runner-up over hurdles at Taunton (2m3f) in his final run in 2025; returns after 141 days off but he needs watching in market back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Dual course winner and he needs watching in market back on the Flat on his return

runners.name: Lusaka
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner but he's a tactically versatile; six wins on turf (1m4f-1m6f) but he's not easy to predict and has been quiet on AW this winter; now only 1lb higher than for his last success but he still has stamina to prove at this trip and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Six wins on turf but all over shorter trips and he's been quiet on AW this winter

runners.name: No More Bolero
runners.spotlight: Turned things around back over hurdles with a win at Ludlow (2m, good) in November but he was a well-held third in a small field at Sedgefield the following month; bit to prove again after that but conditions should be fine back in this sphere and he's well treated on his close call at Chester last autumn; not ruled out on his return.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but he's on a workable mark back on the Flat and conditions should be fine

runners.name: Peking Opera
runners.spotlight: Listed Flat winner for Aidan O'Brien; made a successful hurdle debut for this yard in early 2024 but it's been a struggle since then under both codes; has dropped a long way in the weights but he's hard to trust at the moment and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Has struggled under both codes for some time and he's hard to trust at the moment
runners.owner: S Packham, A Austin And B Austin

races.race_name: Blackmore Building Contractors British EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Fortunate
runners.spotlight: Well-held sixth of eight on Wolverhampton debut (AW) 11 days ago but she looked inexperienced and should know much more this time; likely improver and she's not ruled for yard that won this in 2023 and 2024.
runners.comment: Finished down the field at Wolverhampton but she's a likely improver on her second start

runners.name: Passerine
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to winners Conglomerate (UAE 1m dirt; RPR 98), Jolt (7f 2yo; 89), Raven's Applause (6f; 77), Conformist (Canadian 6f dirt including 2yo/stakes) and Lew's Skyhook (US 6.5f dirt); dam US 6f dirt winner; yard won the Brocklesby last month and she needs a close look on debut.
runners.comment: 28,000euros yearling; yard won the Brocklesby and she needs a close look on debut

runners.name: Dee's Star
runners.spotlight: 17,000gns yearling, resold 7,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced close relative of 5f 2yo Group 3 winner Steel Bull, out of maiden sister to 5f/5.7f winner Marietta Robusti, half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Three Sea Captains; yard 3-49 with 2yos last year and others look more likely.
runners.comment: Yard 3-49 with 2yos last year and others look more likely

runners.name: Doha Rd
runners.spotlight: 12,000gns yearling; sister to 5f/6f AW winner Colors Of Freedom (including 2yo; RPR 67), half-sister to 6f winner Scarboroughwarning (86); dam 5f winner (including 2yo; 93), out of half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Polar Bird; yard 2-36 with 2yos last year and she could be one for later on.
runners.comment: Yard 2-36 with 2yos last year and she could be one for later on

runners.name: Past Passion
runners.spotlight: 11,000gns yearling; eleventh foal; half-sister to four winners including Ibiza Rocks (7f 2yo; RPR 94), I Still Have Faith (1m-1m2f Flat, 91; 2m hurdle) and No More Thrills (7f AW; 80); dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (98); could be a longer-term prospect on pedigree and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Could be a longer-term prospect on pedigree and others are preferred

runners.name: Seed Ya Later
runners.spotlight: Bungle Inthejungle filly; 14,000euros yearling; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Gossamer Seed (7f Group 3) and Questi Amori (Italian 5f/6f including 2yo); quite a bit to like on paper and she's in the mix on debut.
runners.comment: Quite a bit to like on paper and she's in the mix on debut

runners.name: Ziggy Starshine
runners.spotlight: £16,000 yearling; half-sister to winners Pull The Rug (6f 2yo; RPR 70) and Oldbury Lad (7f AW; 58); dam Listed-placed 6f/7f winner (including 2yo/AW; 99), half-sister to useful 1m/1m2f winner Tobar Na Gaoise; yard 0-6 with 2yos this season and she could be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Yard 0-6 with 2yos this season and she could be one for further down the line

runners.name: Madeleine
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 5.7f/6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 92), half-sister to winners Jacob Black (useful 7f/1m), Blood Moon (5f/6f including 2yo) and Ta Ajaab (Swedish 6f), out of 5f 2yo winner; plenty to like on paper and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 30,000gns yearling; plenty to like on paper and she's an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Global Success
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to Italian 6f/6.5f winner Vento D'Irlande; dam French Listed-placed 5.5f 2yo winner (RPR 81), half-sister to Italian 7f Listed winner Ziggy's Dream, out of Listed-placed 1m1f/9.4f winner; has plenty of speed in pedigree and she needs checking in market on debut.
runners.comment: Has plenty of speed in pedigree and she needs checking in market on debut

runners.name: Miss Moneypit
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to 5f/6f 2yo winner Brightling (RPR 56); dam 6f winner (81), half-sister to winners Rwenearlytheredad (5f-1m), Goldenhurst (5.7f 2yo) and Elysian Lady (French 11.5f), out of half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Langs Lash; has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: Has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide on debut

runners.name: Yahaira
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros yearling; half-sister to six winners including Orazio (6f including 2yo/Listed placed; RPR 114), Ten Pounds (6f/7f; 111) and Suits You (6f/7f including 2yo Listed/France/HK; 103); dam 7f AW winner (98); yard 15% with 2yos last year and she's in the mix on debut.
runners.comment: Well-related filly and yard was 15% with 2yos last year; in the mix on debut

runners.name: Nevernotrememberu
runners.spotlight: 2,000gns yearling; never dangerous when in midfield in the Brocklesby (good to soft; 25-1) and she needs to leave that form well behind.
runners.comment: Never got involved in the Brocklesby and she needs to leave that form well behind

runners.name: Devon Angel
runners.spotlight: Got upset in the preliminaries before finishing a well-held seventh of ten on C&D debut (good; 22-1) two weeks ago; passed over.
runners.comment: Got upset in the preliminaries before finishing a well-held seventh of ten on C&D debut

races.race_name: MJ Church Handicap

runners.name: Safari Dream
runners.spotlight: Five of his six wins (5f/6f) have been on turf but he returned after five months off with a fair third at Kempton (7f) three weeks ago; should benefit from that run and he has possibilities on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Five-time turf winner who ran well on his reappearance at Kempton; in the mix back in trip

runners.name: Hunky Dory
runners.spotlight: Won at Southwell for Mick Appleby in December and he made it 2-4 in cheekpieces with a dominant display at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last week; record of 0-7 on turf but he ran well in big-field handicap at York last summer and he's a major player if he can transfer his progress back to this sphere; respected under a penalty.
runners.comment: Made it 2-4 in cheekpieces with an emphatic win at Wolverhampton; respected under penalty

runners.name: Justcallmepete
runners.spotlight: Nine-time AW winner who won three in a row during the winter and was a creditable fifth in Class 3 at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack) two weeks ago; record of 1-21 on turf but he's on a lower mark in this sphere and he had a close call at Windsor last autumn; dangerous.
runners.comment: AW specialist but he's on a lower mark on turf and has possibilities back in this sphere

runners.name: Arabian Cobra
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a heavy defeat in the Ayr Bronze Cup but he did well for Hugo Palmer last summer including a brave win off this mark at Chester (6f, good); returns in new headgear for new yard but he went close on his reappearance last term and he needs watching in market.
runners.comment: Returns for new yard but he's on workable mark and needs watching in market; new headgear

runners.name: Moe's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D winner who scored on her reappearance at Wolverhampton last month and was a close third there 18 days ago; on same mark as for that last run and she's respected back at this track; handles most ground but her turf wins have been on good to firm/firm.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner who scored at Wolverhampton last month; respected back on turf

runners.name: Mumayaz
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who won four times last year including off 4lb higher at Brighton (7f, good to firm); two solid efforts on AW since rejoining Tony Carroll last month and he could be a big factor if he gets the breaks back on turf; tongue-tie and blinkers are removed but he has won without headgear.
runners.comment: Won four times last year and he has possibilities if this sets up for his closing style

runners.name: Connie's Rose
runners.spotlight: All ten wins have been on turf and latest was at Chepstow last September; handles most ground but she ended last season on a low note and she didn't shine on her reappearance last spring; market should guide after 179 days off.
runners.comment: All ten wins have been on turf and she handles most ground; market informative on return

runners.name: Jax Edge
runners.spotlight: Six-time turf winner who has a good record at Bath and is only 2lb higher than for her win here (5f, heavy) last autumn; returns after 165 days off but she went close on her reappearance last season and that was on good to firm at Chepstow (6f); in the mix.
runners.comment: Dual course winner who is versatile ground-wise and she needs a close look on return

runners.name: Aces Wild
runners.spotlight: Won at Chester last summer but that was off 5lb lower and he was never involved here (5f. good to firm) in his final run last season; usually seen at a lower level and he has some work to do after 199 days off; one of two runners for yard.
runners.comment: Usually seen at a lower level and he has some work to do after 199 days off

runners.name: Mr Lightside
runners.spotlight: Both wins were on turf in 2024 but he's well treated on old form and has run respectably on Tapeta (5f/6f) in last two starts; can make the running and it wouldn't be a surprise if he made a bold bid back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Well treated on old form and it wouldn't be a surprise if he made a bold bid back on turf

runners.name: Strike
runners.spotlight: Four-time turf winner but his last success was in July 2024; well handicapped on his best form last season but he's not easy to predict and was well held on his seasonal return last April; some reservations.
runners.comment: His last win was in 2024 and was well held on his reappearance last April; reservations
runners.owner: S Emmet And Miss R Emmet

races.race_name: Wight Event Toilets Hire Handicap

runners.name: Akabusi
runners.spotlight: Drawn widest when a close fourth at Lingfield on Good Friday but still enjoyed a much better trip than third-placed Alvin; may struggle to reverse those placings.
runners.comment: Just behind Alvin at Lingfield two weeks ago; vulnerable to less exposed rivals

runners.name: Marcellinus
runners.spotlight: Recorded an RPR of 71 in each of his three 2yo runs; gelded during his absence; looks on a tough opening mark.
runners.comment: Similar form in three 2yo runs; improvement essential now handicapping

runners.name: Alvin
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of a 5f novice at Wolverhampton in February; plenty went wrong on his handicap debut at Southwell and it was a mighty effort when a close third at Lingfield on Good Friday (6f) given he was hampered at the start, unsettled as a result and then forced extremely wide throughout; surely well handicapped and displayed promise on good to firm on debut.
runners.comment: Shaped with huge promise at Lingfield 14 days ago; must be well treated judged on that run

runners.name: Noble Vow
runners.spotlight: Steady progress with each of his four 2yo runs, beaten a nose a Wolverhampton in October (6f); could progress further now handicapping.
runners.comment: Improved his RPR with each of his 2yo runs, beaten a nose on latest start; handicap debut

runners.name: Wedonttelllies
runners.spotlight: He beat 13 rivals when winning a Kempton novice (7f) last September; beaten favourite but ran creditably on his nursery debut at Newmarket when last seen in October; gelded since; drops in trip for his reappearance and with a good 7lb claimer enlisted.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on nursery debut (7f) but ran okay; down in trip for this return; gelded

runners.name: Grey Horizon
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins for this yard in the winter; made a bold bid from the front at Lingfield on Good Friday before weakening into sixth; his two best turf runs came on soft and good.
runners.comment: Had a good year already but vulnerable to less exposed rivals back on turf

runners.name: Blue Orbit
runners.spotlight: Returned from seven months off (gelded during his absence) with a dead-heat for second at Wolverhampton last month (6f); his win came on good to firm and this extended 5f should suit; not discounted.
runners.comment: 5f turf win last June; encouraging seasonal return last month; not ruled out

runners.name: Concert
runners.spotlight: Didn't really progress for William Haggas after winning a 6f Yarmouth maiden on her second start; late headway into fourth on her stable debut at Wolverhampton last month; better can be expected back up in trip today.
runners.comment: Stayed on for fourth on recent stable debut; stepping back up in trip looks  a plus

runners.name: Coyy
runners.spotlight: Promise on turf last spring; returned from a four-month break with two disappointing efforts in nurseries in the autumn; had a wind operation since last in action and still has time to deliver on that early promise.
runners.comment: Struggled in two nurseries last autumn but promise last spring and he's had a wind op

runners.name: Starmade
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three runs last autumn, getting off the mark when switched to AW at Newcastle in October (5f); his opening mark looks a tough one but he is at least unexposed.
runners.comment: 5f AW win when last seen in October; not obviously well treated but he is unexposed

runners.name: Campenaerts
runners.spotlight: Ended his time with Archie Watson in good form, winning a couple of small-field 5f nurseries; sold for 21,000gns in October; in deeper waters for his seasonal/stable debut.
runners.comment: On the up when last seen but in small-field 5f nurseries; this is tougher on stable debut

runners.name: Red Snapper
runners.spotlight: Close second over C&D in September (good to firm) before digging deep to win a 5f nursery back here three weeks later (heavy); up in class for his return.
runners.comment: Two good runs here in the autumn but he is upped in class for his return

runners.name: Quantum Power
runners.spotlight: His maiden win came on soft ground and he is untried on faster than good; fourth to Akabusi when last seen in October suggests he's vulnerable, and he has an inexperienced apprentice booked.
runners.comment: Maiden win came on soft; not obviously well treated; apprentice having first ride

runners.name: Carefree Dream
runners.spotlight: Won small-field 5f handicap on soft ground here last September; stiff task at Goodwood 11 days later; absent since and looks on a tough mark.
runners.comment: Course winner (5f, soft) last September but returns from an absence off a tough mark

races.race_name: Unibet Supports Bath Racecourse Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ourbren
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden but she finished placed in a fillies' Listed race at Newbury (7f, soft) in the autumn and wasn't beaten far in a conditions event at Kempton (1m, AW) on her return last month; sets a useful standard back in calmer waters and she should be hard to beat.
runners.comment: 0-6 but she sets a useful standard back in much calmer waters and should be hard to beat

runners.name: Tough Date
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on 1m Southwell debut last month but he showed promise when third of nine over same C&D nine days later; this is a much tougher assignment but he has a good pedigree and is open to more progress on this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Showed some promise when third at Southwell and he's open to more progress

runners.name: Malakai Kite
runners.spotlight: 3,500euros foal, 7,000euros yearling, 11,000gns breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; dam placed 1m2f 2yo (RPR 73), closely related to Australian 1m2f Group 3 winner Mask Of Time, out of 14.5f winning half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Miss France; this is a tough starting point and he's probably best watched.
runners.comment: 11,000gns breeze-up 2yo; this is a tough starting point and he's probably best watched

runners.name: Miss Mambo
runners.spotlight: No progress in her three runs at up to 1m and has a best RPR of 55; minor handicaps will be more suitable after this and she's best watched.
runners.comment: No progress in three runs at up to 1m and minor handicaps will be more suitable

runners.name: Ocean Force
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and he finished a well-held seventh of 11 on Kempton debut (1m, AW) last week; opposable.
runners.comment: 150-1 and he finished a well-held seventh of 11 on Kempton debut (1m, AW) last week

runners.name: Lamlash
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in two Flat maidens (1m, AW/good) for Stan Moore in the autumn and was pulled up on his stable/hurdle debut in December.
runners.comment: Finished last in two Flat maidens and was pulled up on hurdle/stable debut in December
runners.owner: Mr H Maye and The Joe Tickle Racing Club

races.race_name: Silvershine Turf Services Handicap

runners.name: Armstrong
runners.spotlight: Back from seven months off (also gelded) with a promising second of nine on his handicap debut at Kempton (1m) last month; up 1lb but he can go well again with further progress on the cards.
runners.comment: Promising second at Kempton last month; can go well again with progress on the cards

runners.name: Silver State
runners.spotlight: Advanced his form in a visor when bagging a pair of 1m handicaps at Wolverhampton and Newcastle in February; has continued in good form, third in 1m Southwell handicap 48 days ago; must enter calculations.
runners.comment: In good form, third in 1m Southwell handicap 48 days ago; must enter calculations

runners.name: Tewkesbury
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward on his return when second at Chelmsford (1m) but failed to build on it when eighth of nine in 1m Southwell handicap 15 days ago; slowly away there and could still do better on his first run on turf.
runners.comment: Eighth in Southwell handicap latest; slowly away there and could do better on turf debut

runners.name: Foothold
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on earlier promise on his handicap debut when a keen-going fifth of six at Lingfield (1m) 78 days ago; hood goes on now after a break and he remains with potential.
runners.comment: Keen-going fifth at Lingfield 78 days ago; hood on now and he remains with potential

runners.name: Glasgow Kiss
runners.spotlight: Has shaped with some promise for her new stable (with Alice Haynes for debut) when fourth in a pair of 7f novices at Southwell and Wolverhampton in 2026; can take a step forward now handicapping up in trip.
runners.comment: 4th at Southwell/Wolverhampton in 2026; can take step forward now handicapping up in trip

runners.name: Halla Bil Zain
runners.spotlight: Placed on four runs (7f/1m) for Owen Burrows at 2yrs; changed hands for 25,000gns and gelded but failed to beat a rival on his stable debut in a 7f Doncaster novice 19 days ago.
runners.comment: Placed for Owen Burrows at 2yrs; failed to beat rival on yard debut at Doncaster on return

runners.name: Chapman's Peak
runners.spotlight: Upwardly-mobile juvenile who bagged 7f/1m nurseries at Kempton in October; remains on a workable mark despite a 4lb rise and rates a player in his hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: Bagged 7f/1m nurseries at Kempton in October; remains on a workable mark so he's a player

runners.name: Crafty Blue
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of her debut when winning a 7f Southwell novice in September; off since but she has more to offer for her in-form yard on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won 7f Southwell novice in September; more to offer for in-form yard on her handicap debut

runners.name: Atalanta Mist
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when third in 7f novice at Salisbury on her debut but she proved a big disappointment after, ninth of ten in 7f Chelmsford novice on final run in 2025; this switch to handicaps needs to spark major progress.
runners.comment: Debut Salisbury third; a big disappointment after so big step foward needed on h'cap debut

runners.name: Grand Pier
runners.spotlight: Modest juvenile maiden who failed to improve on his nursery debut when fourth of six at Kempton (1m) in October; more is needed if he's to get off the mark at the fifth attempt here.
runners.comment: Failed to improve with Kempton fourth in October; more needed if he's to get off the mark

runners.name: Trio
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the third attempt in 1m Leicester maiden in October and backed it up with a good third of six in 1m1f Newmarket nursery following month; she needs considering on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Good third in 1m1f Newmarket nursery in November; needs considering on her seasonal return
runners.owner: M H Dixon & J L Rowsell

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Racing Betbuilder Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: My Ambition
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D winner (good/firm) who returned after six months off with a front-running second on AW at Lingfield (1m) last month; on unchanged mark and he's an interesting contender back at this track.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner who reappeared with a good second on AW last month; respected

runners.name: Galaxy Wonder
runners.spotlight: Won on turf in Ireland last summer and he has form figures of 3143231 on AW since joining his current yard; up another 4lb but he knuckled down well at Wolverhampton last time and he remains well handicapped on his best form; key player in his bid for a double.
runners.comment: Turf winner who has a record of 3143231 on AW for current yard; key player

runners.name: Oasis Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-27 but her win came over C&D (firm) last summer and she returned from a break with a fair fifth (1m, good) here two weeks ago; has claims if she can build on that and she won't mind what the weather does.
runners.comment: Record of 1-27 but her win came over C&D and she ran respectably here two weeks ago

runners.name: Rating
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when awarded a race at Wolverhampton (9.4f, Tapeta) in December but she's finished a well-held eighth there in both subsequent runs; overall record is now 1-24 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in December but she's been disappointing since and is now 1-24

runners.name: Eye Of The Water
runners.spotlight: Some fair efforts on AW during the winter and he hit the frame plenty of times on turf last year; hard to rule out a good run after a short break but he's not won since 2023 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Plenty of decent efforts last year but his last win was in 2023; others preferred

runners.name: Always Fearless
runners.spotlight: Readily saw off eight rivals at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month but he's been disappointing in both subsequent runs; has something to prove again and he has form figures of 5097 at Bath.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton last month but he's been disappointing in both subsequent runs

runners.name: Pitney
runners.spotlight: His last win was in September 2024 but that was off 19lb higher at Chester, and he ran well to finish third at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month; still lightly raced on turf and has possibilities if he handles the conditions back in this sphere (best form has been on soft ground).
runners.comment: Ran well at Wolverhampton last month and he's in the mix back on turf

runners.name: Fact Or Fable
runners.spotlight: Eight-time turf winner including three over C&D (good to firm/firm); well handicapped on his best form in 2025 but he's not easy to predict and he ended last season on a low note; market should guide after 169 days off.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner and he needs watching in market after 169 days off
runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

runners.name: Dappled Light
runners.spotlight: Completed a double off 2lb higher over C&D (firm) last May but he's not hit the same level in five runs since; has bit to prove after 171 days off and he doesn't have a great record when fresh; visor is removed and cheekpieces are reapplied.
runners.comment: C&D winner but he doesn't have a great record when fresh and this is competitive

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Racing Betbuilder Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Eutropia
runners.spotlight: Record of 533313 for new stable and that latest third was a creditable effort from off the pace at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month; on same mark here and she's a big player if she can continue the good work back on turf.
runners.comment: In good form on AW for new yard and she's respected back on turf

runners.name: Annexation
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner, with six on the Flat and two over hurdles; had some struggles during the winter but he showed signs of a revival at Wolverhampton (fourth of 12) last month and is now 5lb lower than for his win at Nottingham in the autumn (1m2f); handles most ground and has possibilities if he gets a good pace back at this trip.
runners.comment: Showed signs of a revival at Wolverhampton and has claims if he can build on that

runners.name: Judge Frank
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row on turf in September 2024 but he's drawn a blank since and was well held on C&D reappearance (good) 12 days ago; may have needed that run but he's hard to predict and others are more convincing; blinkers reapplied.
runners.comment: Both wins were in 2024 and was well held on reappearance over C&D 12 days ago

runners.name: Study Up
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo; won on debut at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) in July 2024 but he's not gone on from that and has record of 839837 in handicaps; remains unexposed on turf but he needs to raise his game back in this sphere.
runners.comment: 5yo who has not progressed and has form figures of 839837 in handicaps; down the list

runners.name: Too Much Trevor
runners.spotlight: Reached the frame on numerous occasions last year but he's still a maiden after 27 attempts and he doesn't have a great record when fresh; opposable back from 201 days off.
runners.comment: 27-race maiden and he looks opposable back from 201 days off

runners.name: Havana Tobouggaloo
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly who was beaten 5l or more in seven runs (1m-1m2f) for Stuart Kittow last season; returns for new trainer after 163 days off and she needs improvement back on turf.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden and she needs improvement on return for new trainer

runners.name: Ajrad
runners.spotlight: Won four Class 6 handicaps (7f-1m) in 2025, including on good to firm and heavy ground; back on last winning mark but he's been out of sorts in last four runs and needs a major revival back on turf; cheekpieces removed.
runners.comment: Back on last winning mark but he needs a major revival back on turf; cheekpieces removed

runners.name: Weston Court
runners.spotlight: Both wins wins were in the summer of 2024 but the first was over C&D and he's well treated on that form; struggled on AW during the winter but he's never shown much in that scene and he needs watching in market back at this track.
runners.comment: Dual turf winner who is on a dangerous mark back in this sphere; needs watching market

runners.name: Kev
runners.spotlight: Went close in a Wolverhampton maiden (7f, AW) in December 2024 but he's struggled since including in three handicaps this winter; still lightly raced but he has plenty to prove on turf debut; visor added.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden who has struggled in his three handicaps; lots to prove with visor added

races.race_name: Sandygate Inn, Kingsteignton 'National Hunt' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: October Hill
runners.spotlight: Is 0-10 under rules; tongue tied when a creditable second of 12 in 2m Ludlow novice last month; cheekpieces are added now and she holds good form claims.
runners.comment: Is 0-10 under rules but a creditable second at Ludlow latest; she holds good form claims

runners.name: Granpa Ger
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden but in good nick for his new stable, fifth of 13 in 2m Ludlow novice in November; no forlorn hope after a break.
runners.comment: Is 0-21 but a solid fifth in 2m Ludlow novice in November; no forlorn hope after a break
runners.owner: David W Jagger & Mrs Belinda Clarke

runners.name: Our Guide
runners.spotlight: Bought for £82,000 after landing Galway bumper on his debut last October; 2-1, made a promising start over hurdles when fifth of 15 in 2m Wetherby novice last month, nearest finish; capable of better for leading yard and a big player.
runners.comment: Promising hurdling debut fifth at Wetherby last month; a big player for leading yard here

runners.name: Oscar Jack
runners.spotlight: 250-1, last of 12 in bumper at Wincanton on his debut last month; cheekpieces are quickly reached for on his first go over hurdles.
runners.comment: Last in bumper at Wincanton on his debut last month; cheekpieces added for hurdles debut
runners.owner: Mrs C Vogt And J Frost

runners.name: Getone
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo; hooded and tongue tied when a remote third of eight in 2m2f novice here for his new yard recently; lots more is required.
runners.comment: Remote third in 2m2f novice here for his new yard recently; lots more is required

races.race_name: New Inn, Alphington, Exeter Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Green Sky
runners.spotlight: 9.5f Flat winner (good) when with Joseph O'Brien; 0-13 over hurdles but has been placed several times at around 2m; beaten 2l into fourth at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) latest; drops 1lb and will have a bit to prove stamina-wise over this trip on this stiff track.
runners.comment: 9.5f Flat winner; beaten just 2l latest but will have a bit to prove stamina-wise.

runners.name: Imperial Jade
runners.spotlight: Triple winner over hurdles at up to 2m3f and goes well on a sound surface; 2lb lower than when last winning at Huntingdon 13 months ago; well below that level over fences/hurdles this season and down the field in 1m3f AW novice event in January when last seen; handy mark if revived.
runners.comment: Triple hurdle winner at up to 2m3f; acts on a sound surface; below par lately; handy mark.

runners.name: Stinginhisstep
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner over a similar trip at Newton Abbot (both good); was competitive from this mark on his last two starts at that track (both 2m5f) and should be involved if straight enough after a six-month break.
runners.comment: Two-time winner over a similar trip (good); should be involved if ready after a break.
runners.owner: A Leach And D V Gardner

runners.name: This Sway
runners.spotlight: Made all for a 2m hurdle win (soft) under Tom Broughton at Southwell on his penultimate start; up 7lb when weakening out of it at Chepstow (2m, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces); drops 1lb; goes back up in trip with more needed.
runners.comment: 2m hurdle winner (soft); held when up 7lb latest; drops 1lb; back up in trip; more needed.

runners.name: Taritino
runners.spotlight: Multiple Flat winner at up to 1m6f and dual hurdles winner at up to 2m3f; goes well on a sound surface; 6lb higher than when scoring at Taunton on his penultimate start; beaten more than 9l when unpenalised at Plumpton latest, but should be competitive.
runners.comment: Multiple winner across both codes; ground suits; 6lb higher than last win; competitive.

runners.name: Mr Jukebox
runners.spotlight: Placed in a good-ground bumper on debut last year; not devoid of promise in three starts over hurdles; longer trip at this stiff track should help to bring his stamina into play, from what looks a fair mark on handicap debut; interesting.
runners.comment: Longer trip should help to bring his stamina into play on handicap debut; interesting.

runners.name: Rangatira Jack
runners.spotlight: 2m3f chase winner here (good) 12 months ago; 0-6 over hurdles; well beaten over fences on his last four starts; bit to prove back hurdling.
runners.comment: 2m3f chase winner here; well beaten over fences recently; bit to prove back hurdling.
runners.owner: Mrs D Robinson Mrs & Mrs Livesey-Van Dorst

runners.name: Asian Spice
runners.spotlight: Three of her four wins have come here on good or good to soft, including over this trip; well treated on her form of late 2024; has been well below that level since over both hurdles and fences; back down in grade for this return to hurdles but there's a bit to prove at present.
runners.comment: Three wins here; down in grade for return to hurdles but has a bit to prove at present.

runners.name: Mrs Pen
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds and well beaten in all three qualifying starts; much more needed even from this lowly mark on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten all three starts; much more needed even from this mark on handicap debut.

runners.name: Anothermanicmonday
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and well beaten in all three qualifying starts over hurdles; watching brief advised.
runners.comment: Huge odds and well beaten in all three qualifying starts over hurdles; watching brief.

races.race_name: Half Moon, Clyst St Mary Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Watchoutitscookie
runners.spotlight: 2m4f hurdle and 2m7f chase winner (both good to soft); versatile regarding going but yet to race on good to firm; on a competitive mark if he can shrug off a lesser effort over C&D latest.
runners.comment: 2m4f hurdle and 2m7f chase winner; competitive mark if shrugging off below-par C&D effort.

runners.name: Moonlight Artist
runners.spotlight: C&D (good to soft) and 3m chase winner (good) who is back on that last winning chase mark; has been better in this discipline and is on a handy mark but was unable to take advantage over C&D latest.
runners.comment: C&D winner; better over hurdles but was unable to take advantage over C&D latest.
runners.owner: The Cheltenham & South West Racing Club

runners.name: Teorie
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner over hurdles on good to soft, at around 2m (acts on a sound surface); stays 2m5f; some way below his best since last spring; well treated on form of almost a year ago if bouncing back but has that to prove.
runners.comment: 2m winner; well treated on form of a year ago if bouncing back but has that to prove.

runners.name: Square Du Roule
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last November (good to soft; held up), when 15lb lower; also held up when showing marginal improvement to score at Wincanton (3m, good) latest; up another 6lb but looks a leading player.
runners.comment: C&D winner who scored over 3m (good) latest; up 6lb but looks a leading player.

runners.name: Man Oh Man
runners.spotlight: 0-11 (placed twice at Taunton over 2m3f); inconsistent but better efforts would give him an each-way chance from this mark, if seeing out this longer trip.
runners.comment: Inconsistent; better efforts would give him an each-way chance, if getting this trip.

runners.name: Stage Show
runners.spotlight: Broke his duck over hurdles at the 15th attempt over C&D (good to firm) last October (held up); well below that level next time, when dropped back in trip (heavy) and beaten almost 9l at Wincanton by Square Du Roule latest (3m, good); more needed but not written off.
runners.comment: C&D winner (good to firm); held on last two starts; more needed but not written off.

runners.name: Vengeance
runners.spotlight: 0-14 under rules; sliding in the weights in four starts for this trainer and those efforts suggest he'll need a further favour or two from the handicapper to break his duck.
runners.comment: 0-14 and looks in need of further favours from the handicapper to break his duck.

runners.name: Elfride
runners.spotlight: 2m7f hurdle winner (good to soft); on a lengthy losing run and arrives with plenty to prove from 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: 2m7f hurdle winner; on a lengthy losing run and has plenty to prove from 4lb wrong.

races.race_name: Heavitree Brewery PLC Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Blue In The West
runners.spotlight: Confirmed earlier promise here when getting off the mark in handicap over C&D ten days ago; up 6lb but had something to spare there so merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Readily got off the mark over C&D ten days ago; up 6lb but merits serious consideration

runners.name: Thank You Blue
runners.spotlight: Rattled off a hat-trick at Ludlow (2m) in February but he beat only one off a 7lb higher mark in 2m1f Newcastle handicap six days ago; could bounce back off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Landed hat-trick at Ludlow in February; beat one at Newcastle latest but could bounce back

runners.name: Got A Dream
runners.spotlight: Bagged a pair of handicaps (around 2m4f) at Chepstow and Warwick before Christmas and wasn't disgraced after five months off when sixth in 2m7f handicap here 26 days ago; considered.
runners.comment: Not disgraced after five months off when sixth in handicap here 26 days ago; considered

runners.name: Jack Sprat
runners.spotlight: Course scorer who went freely when a below-par seventh of ten in 3m Wincanton handicap 25 days ago; drops back in trip now.
runners.comment: Course scorer; went freely when a below-par seventh in 3m Wincanton handicap 25 days ago

runners.name: Mammies Boy
runners.spotlight: A fair 2m5f hurdles winner at his best but he's yet to fire for his current yard, blinkered when unseated rider four out in 2m3f Fontwell handicap nine days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Yet to fire for current yard, unseated rider 4 out in 2m3f Fontwell handicap nine days ago

races.race_name: Heart Of Oak, Pinhoe, Exeter Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Prairie Queen
runners.spotlight: 0-19 but placed over both hurdles and fences at up to 2m4f (all on a sound surface); has been competitive from this mark on her last three starts and should be again.
runners.comment: Has been competitive from this mark on her last three starts and should be again.

runners.name: Regal Cavalier
runners.spotlight: 15.5f winner (soft) on the Flat when trained in France; showed some ability on his second start when with Max Young (2m, good to soft) but well below that level since; handy mark if new trainer can revive him.
runners.comment: 15.5f French Flat winner; has shown ability; handy mark if new trainer can revive him.
runners.owner: Quids In Messers Guthrie Bowen & Prosser

runners.name: Celtic Queen
runners.spotlight: Best finish came when a close second in a good-ground Hereford bumper on debut; peak effort came in a subsequent Wetherby maiden bumper (soft); respectable effort when 11l third on handicap debut at Hereford (2m, good) latest; drops 3lb; each-way player.
runners.comment: Respectable effort when 11l third on handicap debut latest; drops 3lb; each-way player.

runners.name: Prized Jet
runners.spotlight: Placed in a 3m hurdle (good) when trained in Ireland; has gone backwards in three starts for her current trainer, both in terms of performance and trip; latest 5lb drop sees her on a handy mark and no surprise if she can take advantage.
runners.comment: 5lb drop sees her on a handy mark and no surprise if she can take advantage.

runners.name: Modern Style
runners.spotlight: Frankel gelding who has failed to show much in either code so far; is on a lowly mark for this handicap debut but only market support would pique interest at this stage.
runners.comment: Lowly mark for this handicap debut but only support would pique interest at this stage.

runners.name: Kates Choice
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in ten starts; drops back in trip for this and that needs to elicit some improvement if she is going to break her duck.
runners.comment: Drops in trip and that needs to elicit some improvement if she is going to break her duck.

runners.name: Senor Diaz
runners.spotlight: Placed in a bumper and a novices' hurdle (heavy) when with Seamus Mullins; has failed to complete in all three starts for his current trainer.
runners.comment: Placed in a bumper and 2m hurdle; pulled up all three starts for this trainer.

runners.name: Monsun Climate
runners.spotlight: Not devoid of promise based on his last two starts, both at around 2m; has had wind surgery since being 25l third at Taunton in January; probably best watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Had wind surgery since 25l third in January; probably best watched on handicap debut.

runners.name: The Padster
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in five starts; best effort came over C&D (good) last November; well below that level on his handicap debut latest; has since had wind surgery and drops 4lb; may be able to get competitive.
runners.comment: Well below par on handicap debut latest; since had wind surgery; may get competitive.

races.race_name: Royal Oak, Ideford, Newton Abbot Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dunstall Star
runners.spotlight: 22-1 and green, hinted at promise when sixth of ten in bumper at Wincanton in January; this son of Telescope must enter calculations with better to come.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise when sixth in bumper at Wincanton in January; must enter calculations

runners.name: Show Your Hand
runners.spotlight: 66-1, showed some promise despite coming in 17th of 20 in bumper at Newbury 27 days ago, prominent to 3f out; tongue tie added; this Passing Glance gelding could take a sizeable step forward with that run under his belt.
runners.comment: Some promise when 17th in Newbury bumper, prominent to 3f out; could take big step forward

runners.name: La Bamba Chica
runners.spotlight: Mount Nelson mare; half-sister to useful winners Quick Draw (2m3f-3m hurdle/chase) and Guy (2m-2m4f hurdle/chase); dam, French 1m7f Listed hurdle winner, is a half-sister to French 2m2f hurdle winner, out of half-sister to French 3m3f Grade 1 chase winner; the market can guide for this debutante.
runners.comment: Mount Nelson mare; the market can guide for this debutante

runners.name: Danza Kuduro
runners.spotlight: 7,500euros 3yo; half-brother to five winners, including Star Walking (bumper and 2m5f/3m hurdle) and Uptown Harry (2m4f-3m hurdle/chase); dam unraced; this Jet Away newcomer needs considering.
runners.comment: A half-brother to five winners; this Jet Away newcomer needs considering

runners.name: Roxy Rocks
runners.spotlight: 25-1, remote fourth of six in Taunton bumper on her debut 24 days ago; this Jukebox Jury mare needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Remote fourth in Taunton bumper on her debut; she needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: Country Pursuits
runners.spotlight: Has offered little in a pair of bumpers at Doncaster and Warwick this year; tongue tie is reached for now.
runners.comment: Has offered little in a pair of bumpers at Doncaster and Warwick this year

races.race_name: Henri Matisse Coolmore Prix Cleopatre (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Arkansas
runners.spotlight: Another of these to have won on debut and didn't run too badly when upped in class afterwards as a 2yo; started off for this stable with a fairly impressive Listed win here 24 days ago over a bit shorter on good to soft, when Warriors Whisper was third; one to beat if in anything like that form today; entered in the Pouliches and the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Fairly impressive Listed win here 24 days ago, when Warriors Whisper was third; top chance

runners.name: Warriors Whisper
runners.spotlight: Bought for a million euros in August 2024 and won by a narrow margin on her debut at Deauville (AW, 1m1.5f) in February before a third behind Arkansas just over three weeks ago having gone from the front; going need more to reverse that form but does have the scope to do so; entered in the Pouliches and the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Third behind Arkansas just over three weeks ago on just second run; capable of better

runners.name: Gilded Prize
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred and shown very useful ability in two AW wins over 1m1.5f at Chantilly, the most recent 22 days ago under today's rider when ahead of two who take her on again; that race wasn't strongly run early, but she was much the best and should uphold form with those rivals if handling turf; in the Prix de Diane but already a general 16-1 shot for the Epsom Oaks.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two AW starts and holds a couple of these; good claims if handling turf

runners.name: Takerya
runners.spotlight: Won on first start last December over 1m1.5f at Deauville on the AW as a 41-1 shot and bettered that effort when 5l third to recent Prix Noailles winner Pearled Majesty 38 days ago on her turf debut (1m2f, very soft) here; back in against just her own sex but is going to need more to land a Group event; entered in the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Third to the recent Prix Noailles winner last time; more needed to land a Group race

runners.name: Blanche De Medicis
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly, notably a Waldgeist half-sister to multiple Group 3 winner/Group 1 placed Alcantor (who is now with Joseph O'Brien); made a good start to her career when winning here over 7.5f on very soft last October but went off 24-1 when 3.5l behind Gilded Prize on her 3yo return; stiff task to reverse that form if the Francis Graffard filly runs her race; entered in the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Won on debut but then well held by Gilded Prize on the AW last time; improvement needed

runners.name: Turbulente
runners.spotlight: Another who didn't have her first outing until February, but won that over 1m1.5f AW contest at Chantilly and then finished third behind Gilded Prize over exactly the same C&D when a 10-1 chance, beaten 3l having made the running; makes her turf debut and possible she'll find opposition for the lead if ridden the same way; entered in the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Went from front when beaten by Gilded Prize on the AW last time; has to improve for turf

races.race_name: CRE Run Farm Conditions Stakes

runners.name: Rb Yas Sir (Usa)
runners.spotlight: Making his UK debut and arrives here fit having been running in top quality staying races in Abu Dhabi over the winter. Outclassed when in Group One company last month but returns to much calmers waters here and should be very tough to beat on these terms. (R27 W4 P7) (OR 104)

runners.name: Upstart Crow (GB)
runners.spotlight: Has been a real improver for current connections and has a great record fresh. Was a narrow second here in the equivalent race last year over a furlong further. Ended the season winning at Lingfield under this rider who he gets on so well with and if the likely favourite under performs, he's most likely to take advantage. (R16 W6 P6) (OR 92)

runners.name: Al Sharid (Fr)
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred debutant who didn't make it to the track in France. Likely to improve plenty for the outing and be seen to better effect when he's handicapped. (R0 W0 P0) (NOR)

runners.name: Jeewan (GB)
runners.spotlight: Stout stayer who likes to make the running, won on Tapeta on his final start last season at Southwell. Badly in at the weights due to the conditions of the race here though so this looks a tough ask. (R18 W3 P7) (OR 77)

runners.name: Hibikinada Du Mazet (Fr)
runners.spotlight: Wasn't beaten too far by Upstart Crow on the turf at Lingfield last season and performed as well as could have been expected in the UK Arabian Derby at Doncaster when last seen. Has joined a new yard and for all improvement is likely this season this looks a tough ask on these terms. (R8 W1 P4) (OR 69)

runners.name: Zizania Du Mazet (Fr)
runners.spotlight: It took a while for her to get to the track last season, and she never really threatened to win. Has joined a new yard and should be more competitive this season but that is likely to come in handicaps. (R8 W1 P5) (OR 54)

races.race_name: Henri Matisse Coolmore Prix Cleopatre (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Arkansas
runners.spotlight: Another of these to have won on debut and didn't run too badly when upped in class afterwards as a 2yo; started off for this stable with a fairly impressive Listed win here 24 days ago over a bit shorter on good to soft, when Warriors Whisper was third; one to beat if in anything like that form today; entered in the Pouliches and the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Fairly impressive Listed win here 24 days ago, when Warriors Whisper was third; top chance

runners.name: Warriors Whisper
runners.spotlight: Bought for a million euros in August 2024 and won by a narrow margin on her debut at Deauville (AW, 1m1.5f) in February before a third behind Arkansas just over three weeks ago having gone from the front; going need more to reverse that form but does have the scope to do so; entered in the Pouliches and the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Third behind Arkansas just over three weeks ago on just second run; capable of better

runners.spotlight: Nicely bred and shown very useful ability in two AW wins over 1m1.5f at Chantilly, the most recent 22 days ago under today's rider when ahead of two who take her on again; that race wasn't strongly run early, but she was much the best and should uphold form with those rivals if handling turf; in the Prix de Diane but already a general 16-1 shot for the Epsom Oaks.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two AW starts and holds a couple of these; good claims if handling turf

runners.spotlight: Won on first start last December over 1m1.5f at Deauville on the AW as a 41-1 shot and bettered that effort when 5l third to recent Prix Noailles winner Pearled Majesty 38 days ago on her turf debut (1m2f, very soft) here; back in against just her own sex but is going to need more to land a Group event; entered in the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Third to the recent Prix Noailles winner last time; more needed to land a Group race

runners.name: Blanche De Medicis
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly, notably a Waldgeist half-sister to multiple Group 3 winner/Group 1 placed Alcantor (who is now with Joseph O'Brien); made a good start to her career when winning here over 7.5f on very soft last October but went off 24-1 when 3.5l behind Gilded Prize on her 3yo return; stiff task to reverse that form if the Francis Graffard filly runs her race; entered in the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Won on debut but then well held by Gilded Prize on the AW last time; improvement needed

runners.name: Turbulente
runners.spotlight: Another who didn't have her first outing until February, but won that over 1m1.5f AW contest at Chantilly and then finished third behind Gilded Prize over exactly the same C&D when a 10-1 chance, beaten 3l having made the running; makes her turf debut and possible she'll find opposition for the lead if ridden the same way; entered in the Prix de Diane.
runners.comment: Went from front when beaten by Gilded Prize on the AW last time; has to improve for turf
